title: Vendor EIN liveness attestation / signer delegation options (PO pending)
preview:
From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@calyxsys.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Calyx Systems Procurement <procurement@calyxsys.com>
Date: Fri, 29 Nov 2029 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: New vendor setup - tax form and EIN

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the onboarding checklist — we're ready to move forward but hit a snag with the W-9. Our finance team ran the file through their vendor portal and it indicates the EIN on the signed W-9 doesn't match their tax database. We attached the signed W-9 (PDF) and a recent bank letter (PDF) showing the legal name and account details.

Can Redwood accept the bank letter as supplemental liveness proof, or do you require a notarized attestation? We can also route signatory authority through our procurement agent if that helps (they're on copy).

Attachments: calyx_w9_signed.pdf, calyx_bank_letter_11-30-2029.pdf

— Marta
Senior Procurement Coordinator
Calyx Systems


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@calyxsys.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 29 Nov 2029 16:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: New vendor setup - tax form and EIN

Marta — thanks for the quick turnaround and for attaching both documents. I reviewed what you sent. A couple of items and options so we can clear the PO hold: 

1) If the EIN on the W-9 is a transcription/formatting issue, we can accept a notarized attestation signed by the person on the W-9 confirming the EIN and legal name. That typically clears procurement portals.

2) Bank letter is acceptable as secondary proof only when it shows the EIN or a government tax reference number. The letter you sent confirms the account but doesn't display the EIN; that's why the portal flagged it.

3) Alternate flow: remote notarization or an escrow-signer path. If Calyx authorizes Procurement Agent "Calyx Systems Procurement <procurement@calyxsys.com>" as delegated signer and provides a signed delegation letter (PDF) plus a KYC photo id for the agent, Redwood can accept the agent's notarized attestation on behalf of the vendor and proceed with PO issuance.

4) If none of the above work, we can provisionally issue a PO with a conditional payment hold and a 60-day verification SLA — but that requires approval from our finance manager.

Please let me know which path you'd prefer and, if delegation, attach a signed delegation letter and the agent's ID. I'm looping in Laura (cc) to note potential SLA/finance gating.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Vendor Onboarding / Procurement Ops
Redwood Inference


From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@calyxsys.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Calyx Systems Procurement <procurement@calyxsys.com>
Date: Sat, 30 Nov 2029 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: New vendor setup - tax form and EIN

On Fri, Nov 29, 2029 at 4:04 PM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marta — thanks for the quick turnaround and for attaching both documents.
> A couple of items and options so we can clear the PO hold: 
> ...

Thanks Ben — appreciate the options. Our preferred path is to use a delegated procurement agent (to avoid delay with our CFO's travel schedule). Procurement has prepared a short delegation letter and scanned government ID for the agent. I'm attaching the delegation letter now and can get the agent notarized attestation via remote notary this week if Redwood accepts remote online notarization (RON).

Next steps from Calyx side:
- Attached: delegation_letter_calyx_agent_signed.pdf (signed by Calyx CFO)
- Attached: agent_id_photo.jpg
- We will upload a notarized RON attestation by EOD Wed, Dec 4 if needed.

Is Redwood able to accept a RON-signed attestation plus the delegation letter to clear the PO? If yes, please advise the DocuSign/RON endpoint you prefer; we'll route it. If Redwood needs an in-person notary, we'll need to request an exception (will take longer).

— Marta


From: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@calyxsys.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2029 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: New vendor setup - tax form and EIN

Thanks all — Priyom here (finance ops). Quick confirmation on Redwood policy and the PO state: 

• Policy: For a delegated-signer flow we require either (A) a notarized delegation letter + notarized attestation from the agent, or (B) notarized delegation letter + agent identity verification (photo ID) plus a bank-issued letter that lists the EIN. A remote online notarization (RON) is acceptable if the notary platform provides evidence of notary identity and the signing session metadata (notarization certificate).

• PO state: We can move the purchase-order to "Issued — Conditional" and allow invoices to be submitted, but final payment will remain in hold until one of the accepted verifications is received and validated. If Calyx can complete the RON by EOD Wed (Dec 4), we can clear the hold and route payment on the next cycle.

Action items for Calyx: provide RON attestation PDF or provide a bank letter that explicitly shows the EIN. Attach the DocuSign/RON link here when ready.

Ben/Laura: I'll take validation once the artifacts are uploaded and update the PO status in Coupa.

Thanks,
Priyom Das
Finance Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

title: URGENT: Arkwell egress degradation — spike in 5xx & token latency
preview:
From: Daniel Park <daniel@arkwell.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Eng Ops <ops@arkwell.com>, Procurement <procurement@arkwell.com>
Date: 2029-10-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Production 5xx spike — urgent

Ben —

We're seeing a large production impact starting ~08:45 PDT: inference requests to your hosted endpoint are returning 502/504 at scale and p99 token latency has doubled. Our SLO alerts fired across multiple clusters; attached is the alert export. This is affecting customers on the live tier.

What we've observed so far:
- Requests to region-us-west-2 show a mix of connection resets and TCP retransmits.
- A subset of requests hang for ~20s before 504s.

We need immediate investigation and a war room. Can you confirm whether Redwood is seeing elevated egress errors or any peering changes on your side? If a temporary mitigation exists (e.g. routing to an alternate peering or model fallback), please advise.

We're escalating internally and our VP may join the call shortly. Please treat this as Sev 1.

Thanks,
Daniel Park
Director, Platform Reliability
Arkwell

Attachments: arkwell_slo_alerts_20291018.csv


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel@arkwell.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Eng Ops <ops@arkwell.com>
Date: 2029-10-18T09:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Production 5xx spike — urgent

Daniel — thanks for the heads-up and the export. We’re on it.

Immediate plan (first 30m):
- Stand up a short war room (joining link in a follow-up).
- Run quick checks on egress peering metrics and our proxy queue lengths.
- If we see sustained egress failures, we can temporarily route Arkwell traffic to an alternate peering and suggest a model-size fallback to reduce token pressure.

Marissa + Irene: can you start egress trace collection and check for any recent peering or ACL changes? Marissa — please share anything you find in the war room.

Daniel — I’ll loop you into the war room and keep you updated every 15 minutes. Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0182

On Fri, Oct 18, 2029 at 09:12 AM Daniel Park <daniel@arkwell.com> wrote:
> Ben —
>
> We're seeing a large production impact starting ~08:45 PDT: inference requests to your hosted endpoint are returning 502/504 at scale and p99 token latency has doubled. Our SLO alerts fired across multiple clusters; attached is the alert export. This is affecting customers on the live tier.
>
> --Daniel


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2029-10-18T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Production 5xx spike — initial triage notes

Quick triage update from infra & routing:

Findings (first-pass):
- Egress metrics: sudden increase in retransmits and TCP resets across the primary peering link to Arkwell's ASN beginning 08:41 PDT.
- DNS: we see a pattern of TTL churn for arkwell-bgp.edge.arkwell.com — many clients refetching within seconds which is causing cache churn on our egress proxy.
- Proxy: our egress proxy queue depth spiked and then partially backed off; a backlog resulted in head-of-line blocking for some long-tail requests.
- Firewall/proxy behavior: there are signs of flow pinning between their NAT and our egress path; flows are intermittently being re-mapped causing connection resets.

Attachments: egress-trace-20291018.txt (packet capture summary + histograms).

Immediate recommendations:
- Ask Arkwell to increase DNS TTL from 5s -> 300s for the affected records while we stabilize.
- Request Arkwell ops to validate MTU / fragmentation settings across the peering path.
- We can perform an interim peering failover for their traffic to reduce load on the affected link (Jonas can execute).

Irene: can you confirm whether a recent ACL change or release touched the egress proxy config?
Jonas: please prepare the peering failover play (we'll coordinate with customer).

If this remains degraded after these steps, we should notify execs and prepare a goodwill offer preemptively.

-Marissa
Sr. SRE, Redwood Inference

On Fri, Oct 18, 2029 at 09:28 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Daniel — thanks for the heads-up and the export. We’re on it.


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Daniel Park <daniel@arkwell.com>
Date: 2029-10-18T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Production 5xx spike — exec summary / ask

Vivek — quick exec summary for visibility and decision:

Situation: Arkwell (production customer, paid live tier) is experiencing elevated 5xx and token latency starting 08:45 PDT on 10/18. Our infra team has identified peering path instability (retransmits + resets), DNS TTL churn on Arkwell's records, and proxy queueing as likely contributors. We currently have a war room in progress and are coordinating with Arkwell Ops.

Impact: Multiple clusters reporting 5xx spikes; p95/p99 token latency doubled; estimated customer downtime window (degraded performance) ~08:45–(ongoing). Customer VP is looped in.

Recommendation / ask:
- Approve routing Arkwell traffic to alternate peering for immediate mitigation (Jonas to run play).
- Approve provisional 20% service credit for the degraded window to offer proactively if asked by the customer.
- Sanction a post-mortem and a customer-facing RCA to be delivered within 72 hours.

If you approve, we’ll execute the peering failover and draft the customer credit email.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

On Fri, Oct 18, 2029 at 10:05 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick triage update from infra & routing:
> (see above)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel@arkwell.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2029-10-20T16:55:00-07:00
Subject: Final update: Arkwell egress incident — mitigation and RCA next steps

Daniel —

TL;DR: Root cause identified and mitigated. Incident is contained; 5xx rates are back within SLA thresholds.

Summary of root cause (short):
- Primary cause: MTU mismatch + fragmentation across Arkwell’s peering edge which, when combined with their low DNS TTL configuration, produced a burst of cache misses and caused our egress proxy queue to enter backpressure. The combination led to connection resets and 5xx for a subset of long-running requests.
- Secondary contributor: intermittent ACL re-mapping on Arkwell's NAT that amplified connection churn under load.

What we did:
- Coordinated with Arkwell Ops: they increased DNS TTL for the affected records from 5s -> 300s and validated MTU across the peering.
- Jonas executed an alternate peering failover at 11:42 PDT on 10/18 which reduced immediate retransmits.
- Infra applied a hotfix to egress routing logic to prefer alternate peers when packet loss > 1% for 3m, and added adaptive backpressure for the proxy queues to avoid head-of-line blocking.
- We ran a controlled soak and monitored metrics; 5xx and p99 token latency have returned to normal.

Attachments & links: draft RCA (rwc-arkwell-rca-draft-20291020.pdf), packet traces (drive link above).

Next steps:
- We will deliver a finalized customer-facing RCA and post-mortem within 72 hours.
- Proposed customer remediation: 20% credit for impacted billing period (pending your sign-off).
- Joint follow-up: 30-min technical sync next week to align on long-lived fixes for the peering configuration.

We take incidents like this seriously — sorry for the disruption. Please let me know if you’d like the credit applied immediately; we can include a short executive note from Vivek.

Thanks for your partnership,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0182

On Sun, Oct 18, 2029 at 10:05 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick triage update from infra & routing:
> (see above)

Attachments: rwc-arkwell-rca-draft-20291020.pdf, egress-trace-20291018.txt

title: Machine-readable export of subprocessor metadata and service mapping request
preview:
From: Elaine Marsh <elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@deltalabs.com
Date: Wed, 2 Oct 2029 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Request: machine-readable subprocessor export + service mapping

Hi Ben,

Hope you’re well. Procurement and our security team are finalizing the data flow review for the pilot contract and we need a machine-readable dump of your current subprocessor inventory and a simple mapping to the services they support. Specifically our checklist asks for: 

- A CSV (or JSON) with one row per subprocessor including: name, country of processing, contract start date, purpose (telemetry, storage, auth, metrics, caching, analytics), whether they handle plaintext or encrypted tokens, KMS/HSM usage (yes/no), and whether there is any cross-border egress.

title: Deckard capacity verification & fallback plan
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Aug 2029 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: Deckard capacity verification & fallback plan

Team —

Deckard (late-stage POC) is asking for a reserved compute corridor for the POC and first-quarter ramp: estimate provided is ~40 A100-equivalent GPUs sustained across a 60-day window (target POC window 2029-10-15 to 2029-12-15). Attached: deckard_mou_summary.pdf and workload profile.

Asking infra + ops to confirm:
- Can we provision a provisional reservation for that window?
- If not, what realistic fallback capacity (region/rate/model) can we offer?
- Recommended soft-hold workflow and TTL for the hold (how long can we keep a provisional slot before it must be released)

Pls reply with a quick snapshot by EOD today. This is a high-priority commercial ask; sales owners (Marcus) and CS (Grace) are cc'd.

Thanks,
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales Strategy | Redwood Inference

Attachments: deckard_mou_summary.pdf (summary), deckard_workload_profile.xlsx (workload profile)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Aug 2029 10:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deckard capacity verification & fallback plan

Aditya —

Quick capacity snapshot (real numbers pulled at 10:30 PT):
- Total available A100-equivalent pools (US regions): 120 GPUs free across pools; 48 GPUs currently in soft-reserve for other late-stage deals (release windows vary).
- Regional breakdown: us-west: 52 free (20 soft-reserved), us-east: 40 free (18 soft-reserved), us-central: 28 free (10 soft-reserved).

Proposed options:
1) Soft-hold (preferred): place a provisional reservation for Deckard for 40 GPUs for 2029-10-15 -> 2029-12-15 with a 30-day TTL that requires a signed MOU or deposit to convert to a hard hold. Operational impact: holds reduce burst capacity for other deals; estimated cost of reservation (opportunity) documented in attachment.
2) Alternative (capacity-constrained): offer 24 GPUs of full A100-equivalent + automated model quantization pack that reduces compute need by ~35% (profiling based on the attached workload). Will require model engineering to validate QAT/INT8 suitability.
3) Multi-region hybrid: 20 GPUs in us-west + 20 GPUs in us-east with cross-region routing and prefix cache warming — slightly higher latency but isolates single-region outage risk.

Immediate next steps I recommend:
- Approve soft-hold workflow and TTL (owner: Monica)
- Request Deckard to confirm POC window and sign MOU to convert to hard hold (owner: Aditya)
- Engage ML infra team to evaluate quantization path (owner: Marcus)

Attachment: capacity_snapshot_20290814.csv (raw inventory export). Happy to run a deeper simulation if you want specific hours/day profiles.

—Rishi
Rishi Malhotra
Director, Infrastructure Capacity | Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Emily Park <emily@deckard.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Aug 2029 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Deckard capacity verification & fallback plan

Emily —

Forwarding Rishi's capacity note. Summary: we can provision a provisional soft-hold for the requested window (2029-10-15 -> 2029-12-15) for 40 GPUs, pending an MOU/signature or deposit within a 30-day TTL. If you can't commit to full reservation we also have two fallback options: a quantized model pack to reduce GPU needs or a multi-region hybrid setup.

Please confirm:
- Final POC dates (if the 10/15 to 12/15 window is still accurate)
- Will your procurement team be able to sign the MOU or provide an initial deposit within 30 days?

I attached our provisional hold request for signature. Link to DocuSign is in the template — if procurement needs a PO number or specialized terms, loop them in and we can expedite.

Thanks — we want to make sure Deckard's POC has stable infra as you ramp.

Aditya Rao
Head of Sales Strategy | Redwood Inference

Attachments: provisional_hold_request.pdf

Date: Wed, 14 Aug 2029 10:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deckard capacity verification & fallback plan

(See Rishi's inventory + options above.)


From: Emily Park <emily@deckard.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@deckard.ai
Date: Wed, 14 Aug 2029 15:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Deckard capacity verification & fallback plan

Aditya / Team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the transparency on options. Confirming: Deckard intends to proceed with the 2029-10-15 -> 2029-12-15 POC window and we want to prioritize the soft-hold to avoid capacity risk. Our procurement lead is on cc — they'll start review of the MOU and aim to return a signed copy or deposit within the 30-day TTL. Deal reference is DEAL-2029-045.

A couple of asks from our side:
- Can Redwood provide a simple SLA addendum for reserved compute (uptime target + failover behavior) we can attach to the MOU?
- If we need to invoke the multi-region fallback, what latency delta should we expect (ballpark ms)?

I'll upload a signed MOU draft this afternoon (provisionally signed by our BD).

Best,
Emily Park
Head of Partnerships | Deckard AI

title: Observability handoff & on-call readiness: dashboards, traces, alerts, runbooks
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Rami <sofia@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Aug 2029 09:12:00 -0000
Subject: Observability onboarding: proposed handoff agenda and timeline

Hi Sofia —

Thanks again for choosing Redwood for the observability layer. Per our last call, I wanted to kick off a short written plan so the kickoff is crisp and the customer's on-call and runbook needs are covered from day one.

Proposed 2-week onboarding scope (high level):
- Week 0 (prep): data access + sample logs/traces, SLO targets, PagerDuty/ops routing details
- Week 1: dashboard buildout (errors, latency P95/P99, throughput), tracing hooks, span conventions
- Week 2: alert routing, escalation matrix, runbook authoring + handoff rehearsal

Key decision items for ACME:
- Primary on-call integration (PagerDuty reference key + escalation policy)
- Data residency constraint for logs/trace retention (any EU-only datasets?)
- Baseline SLOs for billing-sensitive endpoints (list attached as implementation_matrix.csv)

Attachments: obs_onboarding_mou.pdf, implementation_matrix.csv (baseline signals + owners).

Could you confirm the right attendees from your side and whether next Tuesday 8/13 10–11am PT works for a kickoff? If that slot doesn't work I can share a Calendly.

Thanks — looking forward to a smooth handoff.

Ben Carter
Senior Customer Success Manager

title: Standby slice claim & thermal fit check for HorizonLoop late-stage deal
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Jul 2029 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Standby slice claim & thermal fit check for HorizonLoop late-stage deal

Team,

HorizonLoop is in the final contract stage (DL-2029-0743). They want a Q4 POC start window beginning 2029-08-12 with a 90-day ramp. We need to confirm internal GPU availability and place a short-duration standby slice claim for the pod topology they require while legal finishes redlines.

Ask: can infra/ops evaluate the following and place a soft hold (no billing) for 21 days if viable? If not, flag constraints and propose a fallback.

Requested footprint (initial):
- Primary: 6 pods of 2x H100-80GB (12 H100 total)
- Optional fallback: 12 pods of 1x H100-80GB + 6 pods of 2x A100-80GB (hybrid)
- Network: 100 Gbps intra-pod; prefer AZ-local placement with cross-AZ failover
- Thermal requirement: need AZ2 rack thermal bin <= T3 (see attached thermal-bin-map-az2.png)
- Start target: 2029-08-12 (we can shift +/- 7 days if needed)

Why hold: HorizonLoop is asking for a short 'standby claim' while procurement completes the purchase order. We typically do soft holds for late-stage deals when capacity risk could block a close.

Attachments: horizon_q4_poc_capacity_sizing.xlsx (sizing + QPS profile), thermal-bin-map-az2.png

Please reply with: 1) can we place a 21-day soft hold on the requested topology, 2) node candidates or constraints, 3) any required engineering/ops changes to satisfy thermal fit (e.g., reduce rack density or provide migratory scheduling), and 4) estimate of risk if hold denied.

Ben
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

---

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Jul 2029 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Standby slice claim & thermal fit check for HorizonLoop late-stage deal

Ben — quick thoughts from capacity planning:

1) Pod topology: 6 pods x 2xH100 is heavy on contiguous rack allocation. If we insist on AZ-local placement (no cross-AZ vMotion), we need to check AZ2 reserved pool for H100 pairs. I can run the pod-packing simulator but need the expected steady-state token QPS per pod (from the spreadsheet) and peak burst concurrency.

2) Thermal bin: AZ2 has mixed racks. If they require T3 or lower, we may have to reserve 2 racks and limit co-tenancy. That increases opportunity cost. Could we offer a soft hold on an availability class (H100-pair, AZ2 T3-rated racks) rather than exact node IDs?

3) Fallbacks: hybrid fallback (mix H100 + A100) is fine but will affect latency and throughput profiles; we should map models to fallbacks in the runbook. Also confirm whether they accept model quantization to fit into A100s.

Next step: Marissa, can you confirm HorizonLoop's acceptance of hybrid fallback and whether legal needs a hard node list or will accept an availability class? Neha — can you add timeline constraints from procurement? I will kick off a capacity simulation once we have QPS and acceptance of fallback.

-karthik
Senior Capacity Planner

---

From: Diane Cho <diane@horizonloop.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Holt <marcus@horizonloop.ai>, HorizonLoop Ops <ops@horizonloop.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Jul 2029 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Standby slice claim & thermal fit check for HorizonLoop late-stage deal

Ben — thanks for the heads up. A few clarifications from our side:

- We can accept the hybrid fallback (H100 primary, A100 fallback) as long as you document expected perf delta and provide canary routing that keeps SLAs during cutover.
- Target POC QPS per pod (steady-state): 9k tokens/sec; peak burst up to 2x for 10 minutes/hour.
- We require AZ-local placement for data residency reasons for the POC.
- Legal will accept an availability class description for the standby claim — they don't need exact node IDs; just a commitment window and the class details.
- DocuSign link for the SOW is live; we expect signatures within 10 business days of the standby claim being placed.

DocuSign: https://docusign.com/link/horizonloop-sow-DL-2029-0743

Let me know if you need anything else from our procurement or ops teams.

Best,
Diane
Head of Cloud Ops, HorizonLoop

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Jul 2029 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Standby slice claim & thermal fit check for HorizonLoop late-stage deal

Vivek — please treat this as a capacity gating request for a late-stage opportunity. Summary for ops:

- Account: HorizonLoop (DL-2029-0743)
- Availability class: H100-PAIR, AZ2, ThermalBin <= T3
- Hold duration requested: 21 days starting immediately (soft hold, no billing)
- Expected start date: 2029-08-12
- Performance profile: steady 9k tps/pod with 2x 10-minute hourly bursts
- Fallback policy: hybrid mix (A100 allowed) — routing and perf delta to be documented in runbook

Attachments: horizon_q4_poc_capacity_sizing.xlsx (updated with QPS)

Deliverables from ops:
1) Can you confirm whether AZ2 has 6 contiguous H100-PAIR slots or whether we must accept split racks?
2) If contiguous allocation not possible, provide earliest contiguous window and alternative map with expected perf delta.
3) Create a short runbook for cutover: model routing, cache warmup, and fallback routing thresholds.
4) Put a 21-day soft hold on the availability class and return a hold ID + expiry.

Marissa
Enterprise Sales Ops
Redwood Inference

---

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Jul 2029 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Standby slice claim & thermal fit check for HorizonLoop late-stage deal

Marissa / team — short update from ops after inventory check and thermal validation: 

1) Availability: AZ2 currently has 8 H100-PAIR slots that match the H100-PAIR class; however only 4 are contiguous in rack-affined groups of 2. We can satisfy the 6 pods x 2xH100 request only if we accept split-rack placement across two adjacent racks (same AZ) with no cross-AZ placement.

2) Thermal: two candidate racks in AZ2 are T2/T3 rated per the attached thermal-bin-map-az2.png; they meet the <= T3 constraint if we limit co-tenancy to 2 additional non-critical tenants per rack. That constraint will be enforced in the hold.

3) Hold: placed a 21-day soft hold on availability class H100-PAIR:AZ2:T3 (hold id HB-2029-0781). Hold expires 2029-08-02T23:59:00-07:00. Hold notes and constraints attached to HB ticket in Jira: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/ENG-9213.

4) Runbook: ops will publish a runbook (short form) covering canary routing, cache-warm steps, and fallback thresholds by 2029-07-15 EOD. Key points:
- Canary: route 5% traffic to A100 fallback pods for first 30 minutes, monitor latency and error rate
- Cache warm: pre-warm KV-cache with synthetic prefixes (script in runbook) 24 hours pre-start
- Fallback thresholds: 30% token-latency increase or error-rate >0.5% triggers progressive failover

5) Risk: medium — contiguous rack constraint increases risk of noisy neighbors; if legal insists on fully contiguous racks (single rack groups of 6 pods), earliest availability is 2029-09-04.

I'll attach HB-2029-0781 to the deal record and notify network engineering for the intra-pod bandwidth reservation.

Vivek
Head of Capacity Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: horizon_q4_poc_capacity_sizing.xlsx, thermal-bin-map-az2.png

-- End of thread --

Requested footprint (initial):
- Primary: 6 pods of 2x H100-80GB (12 H100 total)

- Thermal requirement: need AZ2 rack thermal bin <= T3 (see attached thermal-bin-map-az2.png)
- Start target: 2029-08-12 (we can shift +/- 7 days if needed)

Why hold: HorizonLoop is asking for a short 'standby claim' while procurement completes the purchase order. We typically do soft holds for late-stage deals when capacity risk could block a close.

Attachments: horizon_q4_poc_capacity_sizing.xlsx (sizing + QPS profile), thermal-bin-map-az2.png

Please reply with: 1) can we place a 21-day soft hold on the requested topology, 2) node candidates or constraints, 3) any required engineering/ops changes to satisfy thermal fit (e.g., reduce rack density or provide migratory scheduling), and 4) estimate of risk if hold denied.

Ben
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

title: Marketplace Sell-With Credentialing: Proof-of-Usage Spike & SKU Mapping for Lumos Analytics
preview:
From: Sara Liu <sara@lumos.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Lumos Analytics Partnerships <partners@lumos.ai>
Date: 2029-06-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Marketplace Sell-With Credentialing: Proof-of-Usage Spike & SKU Mapping for Lumos Analytics

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We had a productive follow-up with AWS and they asked us to provide a concise evidence package for the Sell-With listing: primarily a short proof-of-usage spike (P-o-U) for last quarter and a SKU mapping that aligns our trial SKU to the marketplace metering model.

Quick context: Lumos saw a 3x trial-to-paid conversion during our BigRetail pilot in Q1 and AWS wants to see the telemetry sample plus the proposed SKU to meter mapping prior to certification. We also expect a co-sell entitlements discussion once the listing is approved.

Can you confirm the exact format Redwood prefers for: 
- Proof-of-usage (time range, anonymization expectations)
- SKU naming conventions for Sell-With & Private Offers
- Any specific metering compatibility notes (consumption vs. seat)

If you can share a short checklist and an example CSV or schema for SKU mapping that would get us unstuck. We aim to have the packet to AWS by end of next week (6/28).

Thanks — happy to jump on a 30-min tech sync if that's easier.

—Sara
Sara Liu
Head of Partnerships | Lumos Analytics
https://lumos.ai

Attachments: none


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Liu <sara@lumos.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
Date: 2029-06-20T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace Sell-With Credentialing: Proof-of-Usage Spike & SKU Mapping for Lumos Analytics

Sara —

Really appreciate the context. I've attached a short Sell-With checklist and a sample SKU mapping template (see sellwith_checklist_v2.xlsx). High level guidance below:

1) Proof-of-usage
- We prefer a CSV with timestamped, anonymized event counts (UTC) for a continuous 30-day window that shows a clear uplift relative to baseline. Include source system IDs (hashed).
- Include a one-paragraph summary of the pilot (customer name redacted if needed), conversion metrics, and a short note on data retention policy.

2) SKU naming & metering
- For Sell-With we map a trial SKU to a marketplace "usage-sku" which should be consumption-based where possible (tokens/requests). If Lumos is primarily seat-based, include the seat-to-consumption conversion rules.
- Use simple, hyphenated SKU tokens: lumos.trial-usw.v1 -> lumos.metering.tokens.v1 (example in the template).

3) Metering compatibility
- Redwood supports both consumption and fixed-quantity metering. For easier co-sell evidence, consumption (metered tokens/events) is preferred because it aligns with our telemetry traces. If you choose seats, provide a predictable conversion factor.

Next steps: 
- Can you populate the sample CSV in the attached template and send back by Tue 6/25?
- Karthik — can you confirm whether lumos's proposed event schema maps cleanly to Redwood's metering pipeline (and flag any transformation we need)?
- Olga — would love your take on the go-to-market entitlements / co-sell commitment wording we should surface to AWS.

I'll schedule a 30-min tech + GTM touchpoint for Tue 6/25 at 10:30 PT; link below if that works.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: sellwith_checklist_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Liu <sara@lumos.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
Date: 2029-06-21T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace Sell-With Credentialing: Proof-of-Usage Spike & SKU Mapping for Lumos Analytics

Ben / Sara —

I reviewed Lumos's brief and sketched a minimal mapping. Key technical notes:

- Event schema: The required fields for Redwood's metering are: event_ts (ISO8601), hashed_customer_id, event_type, token_count (int). If Lumos can export token_count per ingest call, mapping is trivial.
- Time window: 30-day rolling window is sufficient. For a spike sample include a two-week baseline + two-week spike if available.
- Anonymization: use SHA256(h.customer_id + salt) and provide the salt for internal auditing only.
- SKU mapping: please use the attached CSV (lumos_sku_map.csv) format: marketplace_sku,internal_sku,unit,conversion_factor. Example row included.
- Edge case: If Lumos's trials are billed by seat, set conversion_factor = average_tokens_per_seat_per_day and we will meter tokens on onboarding.

I attached a short note (metering_notes_kyi.md) with transformation SQL snippets and the sample CSV. If Lumos can drop the sample into S3 (pre-signed) or share via secure Drive link, I can run a quick ingest and return a validated sample output.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

Attachments: lumos_sku_map.csv, metering_notes_kyi.md

- high-level commercial intent (e.g., joint field motion for BigRetail vertical)
- expected launch window (target Q4)
- a named Redwood AE/SE for joint calls (Ben / Karthik are fine)

If Lumos can provide a 1-paragraph customer story (anonymized) summarizing the value and the conversion numbers, that increases the chance of co-sell uplift. Also, please include any pricing guidance or target ARR outcomes so the AWS team can map incentives.

I don't think we need to ask for additional security evidence beyond the normal checklist for this stage (SOC2 summary + contact for security questionnaire), but we should save room in the packet to attach a short architecture diagram.

title: Re: Order Form - Indemnity for Third-Party IP Claims (NovaCorp)
preview:
From: Simon Wells <simon.wells@novacorp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@novacorp.com
Date: Tue, 18 Jun 2029 09:07:00 +0000
Subject: Initial review - Order Form indemnity

Ben —

Thanks for sending the latest order form. Legal ran a quick scan and flagged the indemnity section for third-party IP claims. We have a hard requirement from procurement that the vendor must: (a) defend and indemnify NovaCorp for third-party IP claims arising from the Service as-delivered (not customer modifications); (b) accept liability up to the aggregate order value or a stated cap; and (c) provide cooperation and notice timelines consistent with our SLA.

Two specific asks:
- Can Redwood accept defend-and-control for third-party IP claims that relate solely to model outputs or service code (i.e., not customer-supplied prompts or integrations)?
- If not, are you willing to accept a mutual defense plus escrow/insurance fallback so we can move forward?

We attached the latest order form with our procurement redlines. Hoping to close this week.

Attachments: NovaCorp_OrderForm_v5.pdf (application/pdf)

Simon Wells
Senior Procurement Manager
NovaCorp


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Simon Wells <simon.wells@novacorp.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Jun 2029 10:15:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Initial review - Order Form indemnity

Simon —

Appreciate the quick read and the redlines. I’m looping in Legal and Ops so we can respond with a concrete compromise. High-level response: Redwood generally accepts responsibility for third-party IP claims that are the result of our models/service as-delivered, but we can’t take on claims caused by customer modifications, prompt engineering or customer-supplied data.

Kimberly / Marissa — can you review Simon’s attached order form and propose a firm stance we can share back? Key constraints from our side: we prefer carveouts for customer-generated prompts, a notice + cure period, and a cap tied to the order value rather than unlimited defense.

Simon — I attached our internal checklist and I’ll follow up once Legal gives a specific redline.

Attachments: redline_indemnity_changes.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Simon Wells <simon.wells@novacorp.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Jun 2029 14:42:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Initial review - Order Form indemnity

Ben / Simon —

I reviewed NovaCorp’s redline. Below is the proposed compromise from Legal that we think is reasonable and aligns with our risk appetite: 

1) Scope: Redwood will defend and indemnify NovaCorp for third-party claims alleging intellectual property infringement to the extent arising from the Service as-provided by Redwood ("Covered Claims"). Covered Claims expressly exclude: (a) any outputs or results to the extent they derive from Customer-supplied prompts, data, or modifications; and (b) claims arising from Customer’s customization, integration, or combination of the Service with third-party software.

2) Control & Cooperation: Redwood will have the right to assume defense with counsel of its choice for Covered Claims, provided Redwood does not unreasonably withhold consent to NovaCorp’s reasonable participation. NovaCorp must: (i) provide prompt written notice of a claim; (ii) reasonably cooperate; and (iii) allow Redwood a 30-day period to evaluate and attempt to resolve the claim before NovaCorp can pursue separate remedies.

3) Cap & Remedies: Liability for Redwood’s indemnity obligations will be capped at the greater of (i) the total fees paid by NovaCorp during the 12 months prior to the claim, or (ii) USD 2,000,000. This replaces NovaCorp’s proposed aggregate-order-value approach.

4) Insurance / Escrow fallback: If Redwood reasonably cannot defend a claim (conflict of interest or similar), we will use commercially reasonable efforts to secure insurance or escrow funds to satisfy indemnity obligations. We’re open to further conversation about an escrow trigger but not comfortable with an unconditional escrow requirement.

5) Notice timing: NovaCorp must provide notice within 30 days of receipt of a claim to preserve Redwood’s rights.

I’ve attached a redline showing inline edits and alternative language for the control vs. mutual-defense choices. If NovaCorp needs defend-and-control without the carveouts above, we’ll need either (a) a higher cap, or (b) a premium pricing/insurance arrangement to offset the increased risk.

Attachment: redline_indemnity_changes.docx

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Commercial Contracts | Redwood Inference

title: Request: continuity coverage carveout for EU failover (RTO/RPO)
preview:
From: Raj Patel <raj.patel@fintrust.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: FinTrust Security <security@fintrust.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Jun 2029 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: URGENT: EU continuity requirement — RTO 15m request

Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We have an internal compliance deadline and need confirmation that Redwood can support a narrower failover window for our EU production region. Specifically:

- Requirement: regional failover (primary to standby eu-west-1) with RTO <= 15 minutes and RPO <= 1 hour for production traffic that handles regulated payments.
- Context: regulator asks for documented evidence we meet the 15m recovery for critical payment paths.
- Impact: if Redwood cannot meet the 15m RTO at the hosted baseline, our procurement will push for Dedicated capacity + contractual carveouts.

I've attached our DR requirements and a short compliance matrix. Could you confirm if this is feasible out-of-the-box, or if we need an SLA exception / add-on? Also include estimated uplift in monthly commitment if a warm-standby carveout is required.

Thanks,
Raj Patel
Head of Infrastructure — FinTrust
Attachment: FinTrust_DR_requirements.pdf (PDF)
Attachment: FinTrust_Compliance_matrix.xlsx (XLSX)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Raj Patel <raj.patel@fintrust.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Jun 2029 11:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: URGENT: EU continuity requirement — RTO 15m request

Raj — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Looping in Karthik (runtime/ops) and Stephanie (accounts) so we can give a joint answer. A few quick clarifying Qs to unblock the assessment:

1) Is the 15-minute RTO a hard regulatory requirement or a firm customer goal?
2) Are all payment paths considered critical, or only a subset (e.g., settlement pipeline)? If subset, can you point to the endpoints?
3) Do you expect failover to be automated (DNS/smart-routing) or manual with a runbook?

We'll do a technical feasibility check and come back with options (native hosted, Dedicated, or a contractual carveout and defined warm-standby SLA).

—Ava
Ava Chen
Senior Account Engineer, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | ava.chen@redwood.com

On Mon, Jun 10, 2029 at 08:15 AM Raj Patel <raj.patel@fintrust.com> wrote:
> Ava / Marissa,
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week...

From: Raj Patel <raj.patel@fintrust.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Jun 2029 13:22:00 +0100
Subject: Re: URGENT: EU continuity requirement — RTO 15m request

Ava / team,

Quick answers: regulator mandate is firm for our critical payment processing (PSD2-related settlement). The scope is the payment ingestion and authorization service; we can share the endpoint list (I attached a redacted manifest in the compliance matrix).

We expect automated failover (DNS + health checks) — manual is risky for the compliance audit. We are open to Dedicated if that's the path, but we need a costed path and contract language that documents the carveout and rollback tests.

Link to the audit doc and runbook draft: https://docusign.com/Envelope/ABC123-FINTRUST

-Raj

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@fintrust.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 Jun 2029 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: RE: URGENT: EU continuity requirement — technical assessment and options

Team / Raj,

Quick technical readout after reviewing the attachments and running a short simulation against our EU warm-standby configuration:

- Native hosted baseline: we target failover RTO ~30 minutes for cross-region warm-standby with automated routing; this is because of KV cache warming, model cold-start and DNS propagation windows. Not meeting 15m out-of-the-box.

- Viable approaches to get to RTO <= 15m:
  * Dedicated warm-standby pool pinned for FinTrust in eu-west-1 with prewarmed model instances and reserved KV cache — expected RTO 8–12 minutes in tests. Requires a guaranteed reserved capacity floor and a warm standby billing model.
  * Active-active with sticky routing and synchronous replication of KV caches — technically possible but adds complexity and a higher cost profile; we'd need 2x capacity and engineering validation for the payment path.
  * Contractual carveout: hybrid approach where Redwood provides a documented SLA for failover sequence (warm-standby with scheduled failover tests) and an agreed uplift for guaranteed prewarming windows.

- RPO considerations: we can meet RPO <= 1 hour with standard checkpointing and upstream buffering; for sub-15m RTO we recommend RPO <= 15–30 minutes depending on event routing and idempotency of ingestion.

Estimated cost uplift (very rough, ballpark):
- Dedicated warm-standby reserve: +30–45% of current hosted run-rate (depends on model footprint).
- Active-active: +120–160% run-rate.

I've attached a short tech assessment and a CSV with a sample cost estimate. If you want, we can scope a focused POC (48–72 hours) to validate the 8–12 minute target.

Next steps I recommend: Stephanie to capture commercial uplift and Laura to confirm executive sign-off for any SLA carveouts.

Regards,
Karthik Iyer
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference
Attachment: redwood_tech_assessment.pdf (PDF)
Attachment: sla_cost_estimate.csv (CSV)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Raj Patel <raj.patel@fintrust.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Jun 2029 21:42:00 -0400
Subject: Re: URGENT: EU continuity requirement — commercial proposal and path forward

Raj — thanks for the clarity and Karthik for the assessment. Summary and ask from our side:

- We can offer a formal carveout: Dedicated warm-standby capacity in eu-west-1 with contractual SLA language that defines RTO <= 15 minutes for the scoped payment endpoints, quarterly failover tests, and a confirmed uplift in monthly committed spend.
- Next steps from FinTrust: sign the SOW addendum that defines the endpoint scope and test cadence.
- Pricing: expecting ~+40% on current hosted run-rate for the committed baseline (final number after scope confirmation).

Laura — can you review and confirm if legal/leadership need to review the carveout language before we send a redline-ready SOW?

Raj — if you can confirm the endpoint manifest (or approve the redacted list we received), we'll prepare the SOW addendum and a 48–72h POC to validate the 8–12 minute warm-standby window.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior Account Director, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

title: End-of-tunnel capacity guardrail request — Helix AI (late-stage)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: James Park <james@helix.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 8 Jun 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: End-of-tunnel capacity guardrail request — Helix AI (late-stage)

James / team —

Thanks for the call this morning. Helix is moving very quickly to sign and wants an explicit capacity guardrail before procurement confirms the contract. They asked for a brief internal confirmation that we can hold the requested GPU profile and provide a 30-day ramp guarantee tied to the Q4 commitment.

High-level ask from Helix: 12x A100s (40GB), 1x dedicated pod for inference staging, and a ~15% burst pool for POC traffic between go-live and month+1. They want a written note that we will not reassign those GPUs for 30 days post-activation unless we mutually agree.

Can we confirm inventory and any constraints (maintenance windows, slice rebalancing, other pre-scheduled commitments) by EOD Monday? I attached a quick capacity worksheet and the MOP outline they requested.

Internal asks:
- Karthik: can ops surface available SKU count and any overlap risk with Jan/Feb renewals?
- Ben: anything on the SLA/ops side that would prevent a 30-day soft guardrail?
- Marissa: is finance comfortable with a provisional allocation note (no charge until invoiced) that we can attach to the SOW?

I want something short and transactional we can share with James by Tuesday morning so procurement can finish.

Thanks,
Ava

Attachment: 2029-06-08_capacity-worksheet.xlsx (xlsx)
Attachment: helix-mop-outline.pdf (pdf)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, James Park <james@helix.ai>
Date: Sat, 8 Jun 2029 14:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: End-of-tunnel capacity guardrail request — Helix AI (late-stage)

Ava / team —

Quick inventory check from infra: current fleet (us-west) shows 26x A100-40GB available for allocation today. If we earmark 12 for Helix, that would leave 14 for other commitments. There are two upcoming maintenance windows (June 20 and July 3) that could temporarily reduce available capacity by 4 GPUs for a 24–36 hour window each. Neither impacts the proposed activation date (assuming activation within the next 2 weeks) but it does mean the burst pool availability during those nights is limited.

Recommendations:
- Offer a 30-day soft guardrail with an explicit clause for pre-scheduled maintenance windows (we'll notify Helix 7 days in advance and provide temporary re-routing if necessary).
- Reserve 12x A100-40GB in a dedicated provisioning slice; carve out a 2-GPU buffer within the slice for routing stability.
- Approve a 15% burst pool as a soft promise — subject to the aforementioned maintenance windows and an internal cap if cluster pressure exceeds 85% sustained utilization.

I can get the formal inventory snapshot and a short one-paragraph caveat for external consumption by Monday EOD.

Karthik

[Internal note: possible overlap with Pipeline-OP 2029-052 for Aug ramp; recommend tracking.]

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, James Park <james@helix.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 Jun 2029 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: End-of-tunnel capacity guardrail request — Helix AI (late-stage)

Ava / team / James —

Ops perspective: we can support the 30-day soft guardrail with the following constraints and mitigations:

1) Constraint: we cannot absolutely prevent reallocation if a critical outage requires immediate rebalancing for an enterprise customer with an SLA incident.
Mitigation: we will include an escalation path and a 72-hour notification target for non-emergency reassignments; emergencies will trigger compensation clauses.

2) Constraint: burst pool is best-effort; heavy cross-tenant bursts during maintenance nights are subject to fair-share throttling.
Mitigation: offer a prioritized traffic queue for Helix during their first 14 days (higher priority for queued inference requests) for the burst pool, then revert to standard fair-share after day 14 unless they upgrade to a Dedicated Pod contract.

3) Operational ask: we need Helix to sign a simple "soft-alloc" addendum acknowledging the maintenance caveats and the emergency rebalancing clause. That keeps legal/finance clear and lets ops avoid indefinite cold-reserves.

If that's acceptable I can draft the operational paragraph and the priority-queue SLA text for inclusion in the addendum.

Ben

Attachment: ops-priority-queue-proposal.txt (txt)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: James Park <james@helix.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Jun 2029 15:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: End-of-tunnel capacity guardrail request — Helix AI (late-stage)

James —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Per infra and ops: we can provide the 12x A100-40GB earmark, a 2-GPU buffer inside the slice, and an elevated priority queue for the first 14 days of go-live. We will label this a 30-day soft guardrail and attach the following to the SOW as an addendum:

- Inventory snapshot and reservation note (signed by Redwood ops lead)
- Maintenance caveat (7-day notification, short disruptions possible during scheduled windows)
- Emergency rebalancing clause with 72-hour target and compensation language
- Priority queue for first 14 days (ops-managed)

Next steps:
- James / Helix procurement: confirm you accept the soft-alloc addendum language and the operational caveats. If yes, we'll have a short addendum draft from legal + an ops paragraph by EOD Tuesday and an updated capacity snapshot attached.
- Marissa: please confirm finance is comfortable stamping the provisional allocation note (no charge until invoice) so procurement has something to attach to the PO.
- Karthik / Ben: please send the formal inventory snapshot + ops paragraph to me before 17:00 PT Tuesday.

I will send a single-page note to James summarizing these points once I have the snapshot and the ops paragraph.

Cheers,
Ava

--
Ava Chen
Deal Lead, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0198

Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/4759/DEAL-2029-078
Attachment: ops-priority-queue-proposal.txt (txt)

title: Intermittent throughput collapse — firewall sessions & DNS cache storms (BrightRoot)
preview:
From: ops@brightroot.com
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: High 5xx rate and throughput collapse across eu-us peering

Avery —

We're seeing a sharp increase in 5xxs and a 60-80% throughput drop starting ~07:55 PDT. Traces point at the VPC peering path to your eu-west transit (AS4455) where packets are being retransmitted and responses delayed. We also saw DNS timeouts where our resolvers report TTLs of 1s and a flood of A queries.

Attached: broot-debug.zip (pcap + traces + app logs)

Immediate impact: checkout flow failures for ~30% of users in US-east. We're hearing from product that this is hitting checkout conversions and potentially crossing our SLA.

Can you confirm whether any firewall rule/action, peering change, or DNS config rollouts happened in the last hour? We can provide packet captures on request.

- BrightRoot SRE (ops@brightroot.com)

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: ops@brightroot.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 09:05:22 -0700
Subject: Re: High 5xx rate and throughput collapse across eu-us peering

Thanks — we're on it.

I've opened internal incident INC-20290603-5472 and looped Karthik and Marcus. Initial check of our control plane events shows no intentional peering change. We'll start with firewall session tables and DNS resolver health checks.

Can you confirm the best time window for a coordinated packet capture? Also please leave the broot-debug.zip on the ticket; we'll pull it into the incident timeline.

Avery Johnson
Head of Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com
+1 415 555 0121


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 11:20:10 -0700
Subject: FW: Quick findings — firewall session exhaustion + DNS resolver storm

Avery/Vanessa —

Quick findings from initial triage (summary):

- Firewall session table on edge-gw-3 is at 98% capacity (SYN/half-open spikes) during the incident window. Evidence: fw-session-stats.csv attached.
- Upstream DNS cluster returned misconfigured recursion behavior: one resolver flipped to authoritative-only mode under load, causing client resolvers to retry rapidly; effective TTL dropped to 1s in our logs.
- Netflows show many short-lived TCP connections with small payloads (likely health-check/keepalive churn from a partner). That churn is filling session tables faster than we can age entries.

Short-term mitigations applied (11:05 PDT):
- Increased session table size on edge-gw-3 (+30%) and tuned SYN backlog parameters.
- Temporarily rate-limited external client-to-edge SYNs at the T0 egress (policy applied to suspect AS ranges).
- Added a passive DNS cache eviction throttle and failed over BrightRoot DNS to secondary resolvers.

Remaining actions (urgent):
1) Coordinate with BrightRoot to identify any partner that started aggressive keepalives in that window; they may need to patch.
2) Plan a peering policy change to prefer alternate transit path during heavy SYN storms.
3) Deeper postmortem on why a resolver flipped to non-recursive mode; likely config drift.

I'll join the incident bridge in 15 mins. Attaching fw-session-stats.csv and a quick synthetic traceroute.

-Karthik

On Mon, Jun 3, 2029 at 09:05 AM Avery Johnson wrote:
> Thanks — we're on it.

Attachments: fw-session-stats.csv

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Jun 2029 07:30:00 -0700
Subject: Escalation to Execs: BrightRoot outage risk — potential SLA credit

Marcus/Avery —

Summary: BrightRoot (large retail customer) experienced a throughput/5xx incident affecting ~30% of checkouts over a ~40 minute period on 2029-06-03. Root cause is currently a compound of firewall session exhaustion + DNS resolver misbehavior. We've applied mitigations (session table increase, DNS failover, rate limits) but there's risk of recurrence until config & peering policy changes are implemented.

Impact: Potential revenue and an SLA breach given negotiated 99.9% availability for checkout paths. Customer is requesting an executive touchpoint and written incident timeline.

Recommendation & ask:
- Approve: immediate allocation of extra Dedicated capacity in us-east for BrightRoot to temporarily absorb spikes (one-time charge waived if Marcus approves).
- Approve: outreach from Sales leadership to offer a credit if the postmortem shows platform contribution.

Marcus — please confirm if you approve the credit waiver and temporary Dedicated burst (I will notify BrightRoot with a mitigation plan once approved).

-Vanessa

Vanessa Ortiz
Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: ops@brightroot.com, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Jun 2029 17:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intermittent throughput collapse — firewall sessions & DNS cache storms (BrightRoot)

BrightRoot team,

Thank you for the quick signal and patience while we investigated. Summary of status:

- What happened: a combination of aggressive short-lived connections from a third-party partner and an internal DNS resolver config regression led to extremely low effective TTLs and a flood of queries; this increased connection churn which in turn exhausted the firewall session table on edge-gw-3. That combination produced elevated latency and 5xxs for your checkout traffic.
- What we did: increased session table, tuned SYN backlogs, failed over DNS, applied temporary SYN-rate limits, and provisioned Dedicated burst capacity in us-east while we harden rules.
- Outcome: traffic normalized by 2029-06-03 12:10 PDT. No further degradation reported; we will keep monitoring for 72 hours.

Next steps:
1) We'll run a joint review with BrightRoot next week (propose Thu 2029-06-13 10:00 PDT) — please let us know availability.
2) We'll deliver a full postmortem draft and remedial action plan by EOD 2029-06-07 (attached draft: postmortem-draft.md).
3) We will propose peering policy changes and a resolver config guardrail to prevent regression.

Marcus Lin
VP, Platform
Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

-- Forwarded message follows --
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, Jun 3, 2029 at 11:20 AM
Subject: FW: Quick findings — firewall session exhaustion + DNS resolver storm

(see attached fw-session-stats.csv)
text:
From: ops@brightroot.com
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: High 5xx rate and throughput collapse across eu-us peering

Avery —

We're seeing a sharp increase in 5xxs and a 60-80% throughput drop starting ~07:55 PDT. Traces point at the VPC peering path to your eu-west transit (AS4455) where packets are being retransmitted and responses delayed. We also saw DNS timeouts where our resolvers report TTLs of 1s and a flood of A queries.

Attached: broot-debug.zip (pcap + traces + app logs)

Immediate impact: checkout flow failures for ~30% of users in US-east. We're hearing from product that this is hitting checkout conversions and potentially crossing our SLA.

Can you confirm whether any firewall rule/action, peering change, or DNS config rollouts happened in the last hour? We can provide packet captures on request.

- BrightRoot SRE (ops@brightroot.com)

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: ops@brightroot.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 09:05:22 -0700
Subject: Re: High 5xx rate and throughput collapse across eu-us peering

Thanks — we're on it.

I've opened internal incident INC-20290603-5472 and looped Karthik and Marcus. Initial check of our control plane events shows no intentional peering change. We'll start with firewall session tables and DNS resolver health checks.

Can you confirm the best time window for a coordinated packet capture? Also please leave the broot-debug.zip on the ticket; we'll pull it into the incident timeline.

avery.johnson@redwood.com
+1 415 555 0121


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 11:20:10 -0700
Subject: FW: Quick findings — firewall session exhaustion + DNS resolver storm

Avery/Vanessa —

Quick findings from initial triage (summary):

- Firewall session table on edge-gw-3 is at 98% capacity (SYN/half-open spikes) during the incident window. Evidence: fw-session-stats.csv attached.
- Upstream DNS cluster returned misconfigured recursion behavior: one resolver flipped to authoritative-only mode under load, causing client resolvers to retry rapidly; effective TTL dropped to 1s in our logs.
- Netflows show many short-lived TCP connections with small payloads (likely health-check/keepalive churn from a partner). That churn is filling session tables faster than we can age entries.

Short-term mitigations applied (11:05 PDT):
- Increased session table size on edge-gw-3 (+30%) and tuned SYN backlog parameters.
- Temporarily rate-limited external client-to-edge SYNs at the T0 egress (policy applied to suspect AS ranges).
- Added a passive DNS cache eviction throttle and failed over BrightRoot DNS to secondary resolvers.

Remaining actions (urgent):
1) Coordinate with BrightRoot to identify any partner that started aggressive keepalives in that window; they may need to patch.
2) Plan a peering policy change to prefer alternate transit path during heavy SYN storms.
3) Deeper postmortem on why a resolver flipped to non-recursive mode; likely config drift.

I'll join the incident bridge in 15 mins. Attaching fw-session-stats.csv and a quick synthetic traceroute.

-Karthik

On Mon, Jun 3, 2029 at 09:05 AM Avery Johnson wrote:
> Thanks — we're on it.

Attachments: fw-session-stats.csv

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Jun 2029 07:30:00 -0700
Subject: Escalation to Execs: BrightRoot outage risk — potential SLA credit

Marcus/Avery —

Summary: BrightRoot (large retail customer) experienced a throughput/5xx incident affecting ~30% of checkouts over a ~40 minute period on 2029-06-03. Root cause is currently a compound of firewall session exhaustion + DNS resolver misbehavior. We've applied mitigations (session table increase, DNS failover, rate limits) but there's risk of recurrence until config & peering policy changes are implemented.

Impact: Potential revenue and an SLA breach given negotiated 99.9% availability for checkout paths. Customer is requesting an executive touchpoint and written incident timeline.

Recommendation & ask:
- Approve: immediate allocation of extra Dedicated capacity in us-east for BrightRoot to temporarily absorb spikes (one-time charge waived if Marcus approves).
- Approve: outreach from Sales leadership to offer a credit if the postmortem shows platform contribution.

Marcus — please confirm if you approve the credit waiver and temporary Dedicated burst (I will notify BrightRoot with a mitigation plan once approved).

-Vanessa

Vanessa Ortiz
Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: ops@brightroot.com, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Jun 2029 17:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intermittent throughput collapse — firewall sessions & DNS cache storms (BrightRoot)

BrightRoot team,

Thank you for the quick signal and patience while we investigated. Summary of status:

- What happened: a combination of aggressive short-lived connections from a third-party partner and an internal DNS resolver config regression led to extremely low effective TTLs and a flood of queries; this increased connection churn which in turn exhausted the firewall session table on edge-gw-3. That combination produced elevated latency and 5xxs for your checkout traffic.
- What we did: increased session table, tuned SYN backlogs, failed over DNS, applied temporary SYN-rate limits, and provisioned Dedicated burst capacity in us-east while we harden rules.
- Outcome: traffic normalized by 2029-06-03 12:10 PDT. No further degradation reported; we will keep monitoring for 72 hours.

Next steps:
1) We'll run a joint review with BrightRoot next week (propose Thu 2029-06-13 10:00 PDT) — please let us know availability.
2) We'll deliver a full postmortem draft and remedial action plan by EOD 2029-06-07 (attached draft: postmortem-draft.md).
3) We will propose peering policy changes and a resolver config guardrail to prevent regression.

Marcus Lin
VP, Platform
Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

Subject: FW: Quick findings — firewall session exhaustion + DNS resolver storm

Hi Arielle — thanks again for a focused call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can lock a 60–90 minute technical deep dive (TDX) with your infra/ML leads:

Recap (from our discovery)
- Primary goal: validate serving latency and cost for Clearpath models at 1–5 RPS and discuss fallbacks/cost controls.
- Key stakeholders to include: your infra lead (Daniel), ML platform engineer, and platform/security for data flows.
- Success criteria: confirm baseline latency SLOs, identify quantization/batching approach, agree mutual action plan for a 30-day POC.

Proposed TDX scope & agenda (60–90m)
- 0–10m: quick objectives + attendee intros
- 10–30m: model & infra constraints (your team walks us through prod topology)
- 30–60m: Redwood demo — serving runtime, batching, KV caching, and quantization tradeoffs
- 60–80m: runbook & fallbacks (SLA/rollout discussion)
- 80–90m: next steps / mutual action plan items

Availability — would any of these windows work for your team (all times PT)?
1) Tue May 14 — 10:00–11:30 PT
2) Wed May 15 — 08:30–10:00 PT
3) Thu May 16 — 13:00–14:30 PT

title: Request: standard token economics & eligible model list
preview:
From: Evan Strauss <evan@pebblehealth.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Malhotra <priya.malhotra@pebblehealth.ai>
Date: Wed, 8 May 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick ask: hosted token economics + supported models

Hi Aisha,

We're evaluating hosted inference providers for a customer-facing summarization feature. Can you share your standard per-1M token economics (separate input vs output rates) and a short list of the models you support on the hosted API with their recommended use cases (summarization, chat, embeddings)?

Couple of notes on our side:
- Expected starting volume ~15M tokens/month, potentially ramping to ~120M over 6 months.
- Summaries expand content; outputs could be 2-3x inputs on average.
- We care about predictable unit costs and whether you have a recommended model for low-latency summarization at reasonable cost.

I've attached a short doc with a sample traffic breakdown. If you have a short rate sheet or baseline we can use for early budgetary work that'd be great.

Thanks,
Evan
Evan Strauss
Head of Product
PebbleHealth
attachments: pricing_questions.pdf (doc)

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Strauss <evan@pebblehealth.ai>
Cc: Priya Malhotra <priya.malhotra@pebblehealth.ai>
Date: Wed, 8 May 2029 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick ask: hosted token economics + supported models

Hi Evan — thanks for the context and the traffic sample. Attaching our baseline PDF with standard IO splits and a short model catalog for hosted. High-level answers below to help with budgeting:

Baseline hosted rates (public list / starter tier, no commit):
- Input tokens: $0.45 per 1M tokens
- Output tokens: $0.90 per 1M tokens

Notes:
- We bill input and output separately. Because you expect output expansion (2-3x), modeled cost should assume the output column.
- Small/fast models (use-case: short summaries, high QPS) are cheaper per token but may produce slightly shorter answers; larger models give higher-quality summaries for complex documents.

Short hosted model roster (recommended use cases):
- redwood-compact-1: low-latency summarization, low cost (good for short clinical notes)
- redwood-ada-2: embeddings & light NLU
- redwood-standard-3: balanced cost/quality for summarization and chat
- redwood-xlarge-1: highest quality for long-form summarization and extraction

We've included rough latency / cost guidance per model in the attached PDF. For predictable economics at scale we recommend a committed monthly token tier (see Ben's email below for discounts).

If you can confirm a 6- or 12-month commit range (or a target monthly token band), I can share a proposal with concrete discounted rates and the specific SLA for Dedicated capacity if you need it.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0124
attachments: redwood_baseline_rates_v1.pdf

On Wed, May 8, 2029 at 09:12 AM Evan Strauss <evan@pebblehealth.ai> wrote:
> Hi Aisha,
> 
> We're evaluating hosted inference providers for a customer-facing summarization feature.... (see attached)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Strauss <evan@pebblehealth.ai>, Priya Malhotra <priya.malhotra@pebblehealth.ai>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 9 May 2029 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: RW proposal: baseline + commit discounts for PebbleHealth (RW-20290508-001)

Hi Evan / Priya — looping in to put some numbers on the table. Thanks Aisha for the intro and baseline.

We can offer the following committed monthly tiers (hosted API, per-1M token rates shown):
- No commit (public): input $0.45 / 1M, output $0.90 / 1M
- 6-month commit (>= 50M tokens/month): input $0.36 / 1M (20% off), output $0.72 / 1M (20% off)
- 12-month commit (>= 100M tokens/month): input $0.30 / 1M (33% off), output $0.60 / 1M (33% off)

Model availability on hosted remains the same across tiers; Dedicated capacity is an option if you need an SLA-backed latency target. We've attached a proposed commit-tier workbook that lays out example monthly invoices at your banded volumes.

Other commercial notes:
- We can include a one-time onboarding credit for pilots (ask Aisha for specifics).
- Standard terms are Redwood's MSP; we can accommodate customer PO and 30/45 day net terms for established customers.

Next steps: if you confirm a target starting monthly band (15M or 50M), I'll produce a short commercial summary and term sheet you can take to procurement.

Ben Carter
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai
attachments: RW-proposed-commit-tiers-2029-05-08.xlsx

On Wed, May 8, 2029 at 11:04 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Evan — thanks for the context and the traffic sample. Attaching our baseline PDF with standard IO splits and a short model catalog for hosted...


From: Evan Strauss <evan@pebblehealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Malhotra <priya.malhotra@pebblehealth.ai>
Date: Thu, 9 May 2029 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RW proposal: baseline + commit discounts for PebbleHealth (RW-20290508-001)

Ben / Aisha — thanks for the quick turnaround and the workbook. A couple quick items:

- The 6-month commit band looks closest to where we may land after pilot. Can you confirm whether the 6-month discount applies retroactively to pilot usage if we convert within 30 days?
- Procurement will want a short summary of invoicing cadence and whether you accept a single PO that covers the first quarter.
- We also have a data-security questionnaire (SOC 2 + logs). Can you point me to the relevant security pack or contact to start that thread?

Assuming we can get a short procurement-ready summary, we'll route this internally and aim to decide on a pilot in the next 7–10 business days.

Appreciate the clarity — Evan
