title: APAC+EU edge disruption — mitigations & post-incident action checklist
BrightArc vendor onboarding — W-9 OTP failure and alternative proof
preview:
From: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightArc Accounting <accounting@brightarc.com>
Date: Wed, 1 May 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Hi Ben,

We're ready to complete Redwood's vendor setup so procurement can issue the PO. I attached the signed W-9 (W-9_BrightArc_signed.pdf) but our finance team reports the DocuSign OTP flow failed when we attempted electronic authentication. The error on our side shows the OTP timed out before we could confirm.

Can you confirm what alternate documents you will accept so we can get the PO in your system? If needed we can provide a bank confirmation letter or an IRS CP 575 notice. Our EIN is 12-3456789 (company name: BrightArc Inc.; DBA: BrightArc Labs).

Looking to get this moving this week — we have an upcoming SOW kickoff tied to the PO.

Thanks,
Sam Torres
Director of Finance
BrightArc Inc.
Sam.Torres@brightarc.com

Attachments: W-9_BrightArc_signed.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 1 May 2029 12:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Hi Sam — thanks for sending this over.

Two quick asks so we can progress:
1) Can you confirm the EIN exactly as printed on the W-9 and whether BrightArc Inc. is the legal name on the form (vs the DBA)? The tax team flagged a mismatch in an earlier upload where the DBA showed on the signature page but the EIN on the form matched the legal entity.
2) If the DocuSign OTP flow is failing on your side, we can accept one of the following as alternate evidence (please provide one):
   - A bank letter on bank letterhead confirming account ownership and EIN (scanned PDF, signed)
   - An IRS CP 575 or IRS EIN issuance letter (PDF)
   - A notarized affidavit confirming the EIN and signatory authority (signed + notary stamp)

I copied Kimberly (tax) to advise on the minimum acceptable fields. Monica from procurement will track the PO side. If we can get one of the alternate docs and the signed W-9, we can provisionally create a PO with a hold so the project kickoff isn't delayed.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com


From: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightArc Accounting <accounting@brightarc.com>
Date: Thu, 2 May 2029 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Ben — appreciate the quick reply.

Attaching a scanned bank confirmation letter (bank_letter_brightarc.pdf). Our treasury team pulled it together this morning. Note: the signed W-9 we uploaded uses the legal entity "BrightArc Inc." but the signature block printed the DBA (BrightArc Labs) beneath the signer's name. That was likely why your tax checker flagged the mismatch. The EIN on both the W-9 and the bank letter is shown as 12-3456789.

DocuSign flow: we attempted OTP via SMS to the signatory and the code expired. We tried again and it failed once more; we can coordinate a time window if you prefer a live OTP attempt.

If the bank letter is acceptable, please let me know and we'll move forward. If you need notarized affidavits instead, we can get that done but it will add 2-3 business days.

Thanks — Sam

--
Sam Torres
Director of Finance, BrightArc Inc.
Attachments: bank_letter_brightarc.pdf


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 2 May 2029 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: FW: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Looping Jordan (procurement) and Kimberly (tax verification).

Jordan — please review the attached bank letter and the W-9 Sam provided. Tax flagged a name presentation mismatch (legal name vs DBA). If Kimberly confirms the bank letter plus the W-9 with matching EIN is sufficient, can procurement create a provisional PO and place it in "Pending - compliance" status? We would like to provision PO-72819 for $50,000 to avoid blocking the kickoff, but mark it as conditional until we receive either the notarized affidavit or an IRS issuance letter.

I attached the internal checklist we use for conditional releases (procurement_checklist.xlsx) — follow the steps on tab 2, and note we require the source of truth for the EIN (bank letter or IRS doc) before release.

Kimberly — can you confirm acceptance criteria? If the bank letter includes bank contact info, will tax accept it plus the W-9? If more is required, please list the minimum fields (bank name, account holder name, EIN, dated within 90 days, bank signatory + contact).

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Procurement Operations Manager
monica.patel@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 May 2029 17:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Thanks everyone — quick update for next steps and timeline: 

1) Procurement has created a provisional PO: PO-72819 (link: https://app.redwood.example/po/PO-72819). Status: "Pending - compliance". Amount: $50,000.
2) Tax (Kimberly) confirmed that the attached bank letter plus the W-9 with matching EIN is sufficient for conditional release. Kimberly: please confirm in thread if you require anything else before we move the PO to "Awaiting Release".
3) Sam: if you can provide either a notarized affidavit or an IRS CP 575 within 3 business days, we will remove the conditional hold and complete PO issuance. Alternatively, if you want to retry the DocuSign OTP, let us know a 30-minute window and we'll coordinate a live session.

Sam — once we have one of the items above, procurement will finalize and move PO-72819 to issued. We appreciate the quick turnaround on this so the SOW kickoff isn't delayed.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Phone: (415) 555-0172

> Looping Jordan (procurement) and Kimberly (tax verification).
> 
> Jordan — please review the attached bank letter and the W-9 Sam provided.
> 
> -- Monica

title: Keepsake Health renewal — request for non-standard concessions
preview:
From: Lara Green <lara.green@keepsake.health>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 Apr 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal request and concessions — Keepsake Health (KSK)

Hi Ava,

We're approaching renewal for Keepsake Health (Deal KSK-2029-059) and I wanted to get a formal request on file before our finance window closes May 10. Our board is asking for a more favorable renewal given last quarter's incident and shifting budget timing. Specific asks are below: 

- 40% one-time term discount on the next 12-month renewal invoice
- $60,000 one-time credit to offset incident impact and migration costs
- 6-month term reset (push renewal date from July to January)
- SLA add-on (99.95% uptime warranty with a 6-month penalty/credit clause)

We appreciate the reliability improvements your team shipped, and we want to continue with Redwood, but these concessions are critical for the renewal to go through. I attached a short note from our CFO and usage highlights.

Can you let me know if Redwood can accommodate any of these items, and who on your side needs to sign off? We're happy to join a quick call this week.

Thanks,
Lara Green
Head of Procurement, Keepsake Health
Attachments: Keepsake_Renewal_Request.pdf (pdf)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Lara Green <lara.green@keepsake.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 Apr 2029 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal request and concessions — Keepsake Health (KSK)

Hi Lara — thanks for the clear note and for attaching the CFO statement.

We value Keepsake as a partner and I want to be transparent: the asks you listed are outside our standard renewal authority. I’d like to align internally on what we can offer quickly so we can get a proposal over to you.

I’ve looped in Karthik (AM) and Marissa (VP Sales) to assess concession authority and next steps. Would you be available for a 30-minute call tomorrow at 10am PT to walk through usage and the specific impact you’re citing? If that timing doesn’t work, suggest two alternatives.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0123

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 26 Apr 2029 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: INTERNAL: Keepsake Health renewal — request for non-standard concessions (needs exec/desk approval)

Team — following Lara's request (attached), Keepsake is asking for a sizable package to re-up. Summary of asks: 40% discount, $60k credit, 6-month term reset, SLA 99.95% add-on.

Why this matters: Keepsake is mid-market, steady growth, but we saw churn risk after the outage in Q1. Subject: Marketplace listing + co-sell handoff (Horizon x Redwood)

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We’re ready to move from pilot -> marketplace listing and want to coordinate the co-sell enablement and sell-through plan. Two quick asks to get started:

1) Can you confirm the set of listing artifacts Redwood requires (docs, billing SKUs, SSO evidence, logos)? Miguel is compiling on our side.
2) Horizon prefers a private-offer path for the first 90 days — are there any special metering/taxonomy requirements we should know about for the SKU mapping?

I attached the integration spec and a draft MAP sheet for your intake. If it helps, we can schedule a 30-min intake with your partner ops and technical lead next week.

Best,
Sara Patel
Head of Alliances | Horizon Cloud Solutions
[sara.patel@horizoncloud.com]

Attachments: Horizon_SSO_Integration_Spec.pdf (PDF, 1.2 MB); Horizon-MAP-v2.xlsx (XLSX, 240 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@horizoncloud.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Apr 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace listing + co-sell handoff (Horizon x Redwood)

Sara — thanks for sending these over and congrats on the momentum. Short answers up front:

- Listing artifacts: We need: product description + use cases, technical integration checklist (SSO, VPC/Pricing topology), logos (SVG + PNG), support contacts, and a short co-sell one-pager. I’ll paste our intake template after this email.
- Private offers: We support private offers; SKU-level metering for co-sell requires the meter ID and whether it’s per-hour or per-unit. If you want Redwood to map Horizon SKUs into a bundled offer, we’ll need the mapping sheet filled out (the MAP sheet you attached looks good).

Vivek — looping you in for co-sell entitlement and partner tiering. Grace — can your team run a quick tech-readiness check once we get the SSO spec?

Ben Carter
Partnerships Lead, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0134

Onboarding next steps: 1) short intake call to align on private-offer flow; 2) vendor evidence packet for listing; 3) HubSpot deal update and mutual action plan (we’ll share a link).

- Network diagram for any private link/VPC peering (if you plan to use private endpoints).
- API throttling & SLA expectations for the initial private-offer customers.

For co-sell enablement:
- Product one-pager (PDF) focused on buyer personas and pricing triggers
- A short demo script (5–7 min) we can share with field sellers

Action items before the intake call:
- Sara: please upload SAML metadata XML and a signed SOC 2 summary (or a SOC 2 letter) to the Drive folder in related_links. Also confirm whether Horizon wants per-hour or per-instance SKU metering in the MAP sheet.
- Grace: once Sara confirms, please run the technical-readiness checklist and flag any missing certs or infra dependencies.

Olga Petrov
Partner Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.com

Subject: Pilot extension — request for additional credits + runway

Ben — thanks again for the demo last week. EmberLynx's engineering team is seeing promising results but needs a bit more runway to validate accuracy at scale. Two asks: 

1) Top-up the pilot credits by 150k tokens (we burned through the initial allocation faster than expected due to longer context tests).
2) Extend the private trial by an additional 30 days so we can run the VPC-based integration and finish compliance review.

We're close to agreeing on a 12-month commitment (we're comfortable with a modest MRR starting in Q3) but need the above to finish the proof-of-value. Would you be able to propose an approval path for credits + a short discount on committed rate (something like a one-time 10% ramp discount for first quarter)? Happy to share our internal evaluation notes if helpful.

Also attaching a summary of our usage and token burn (see attachment).

Thanks,
Claire

Head of ML Engineering, EmberLynx

Why I think we should consider it:
- EmberLynx is a high-fit prospect for Dedicated/Private given their compliance needs; VPC integration will become a sticky config if successful.
- They already signaled willingness for a 12-month commit, so this is an activation/top-up ask to get to negotiation.
- Risk is modest; we can make the credit one-off and tie discount to signed PO + DocuSign within 30 days.

Requested approvals:
- Credit top-up: 150k tokens as one-time pilot grant (chargeback to growth bucket if needed).
- Trial extension: +30 days for Private trial (no extra cost).
- Discount: Exec approval required for >5% off list for initial committed rate — they're asking ~10%. If full 10% isn't acceptable, can we consider a 5% ramp plus credits?

title: Warmup gating, queue behavior, and scale-up acceptance — Osprey follow-ups
preview:
From: Claire Zhou <c.zhou@osprey-ai.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Osprey Ops <ops@osprey-ai.com>
Date: 2029-04-11T09:08:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up: warm-pool gating + queueing behavior (post-demo)

Thanks for the demo earlier — quick recap from my side and a few open questions we promised to surface: 

Summary:
- Target latency SLO for our product pages: p95 <= 300ms for short prompts (<= 64 tokens), p95 <= 450ms for typical customer chat flows (avg 128 tokens).
- Peak concurrency expected during sale events: ~2.5k concurrent requests sustained for ~10 minutes, spiking to ~5k for ~60s.
- Streaming is used for chat UI (clients expect first-token < 120ms, subsequent token cadence steady).

Open questions:
1) Warm-pool sizing: you mentioned a warm pool that keeps KV caches ready — what warm instances/seconds do you recommend to hit the above p95 targets for both short and typical flows?
2) Scale-up path: how long to move from 0 spare capacity to fully-primed instances under our account tier? Is there an observable degradation window and expected queue build-up pattern?
3) Queueing and backpressure: if burst overshoots by 20%, what does the steady-state queue latency look like and when will requests be rejected vs queued?

Attachments: sample traces we captured during our internal load test (streaming_trace_sample_20290410.jsonl).

Next steps: please send a short ops-level runbook or bullets we can hand to our SREs so they can map their expectations.

—Claire
Claire Zhou
Head of Infrastructure
Osprey AI


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Claire Zhou <c.zhou@osprey-ai.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2029-04-11T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: warm-pool gating + queueing behavior (post-demo)

Claire — thanks for the clear recap and the trace file. Good questions; below are concise answers and an action plan. I kept it operational so your SREs can enact quickly.

Quick answers (high-level):
- Warm-pool recommendation: start with N_warm = 8 medium GPU instances per region (we'll size by your model variant) with a 90s preheat window. That gives p95 headroom for short prompts; bump to 12 if you see heavy streaming priming penalties.
- Scale-up: cold -> fully primed in ~75–110s depending on model and KV-cache size; during that window you will see queuing described below.
- Queueing/backpressure: our system queues requests up to a per-account soft queue (default 2000 concurrent queued tokens). With your 5k 60s spike example, expect a brief queueing tail; we recommend a circuit-breaker at ~30s request age to fail fast for non-blocking UX.

Detailed ops bullets to hand to SREs (attachment: warmup_runbook_v1.pdf):
1) Warm-pool sizing rubric:
   - Small model (7B): 6–8 warm instances per region
   - Medium (13B): 8–12 warm instances per region
   - Large (>30B): 14+ warm instances or dedicated capacity
   - For your mix (50% short, 50% chat): start at 10 medium warm instances, monitor p95 over 24h, +25% if observed p95 > target.
2) Preheat window: 60–120s depending on KV cache size; default we use 90s.
3) Scale-up timeline and phases:
   - 0–20s: scheduler allocates containers and initializes kernels (CPU-bound)
   - 20–60s: model weights are loaded to GPU and KV cache warm starts
   - 60–110s: final priming, streaming handshake warmups complete
4) Expected queue behavior during spike:
   - For a 5k/60s spike with 2.5k baseline: short-term queue depth grows for ~30–90s, then drains as warm-pool becomes available. Tail p99 may exceed SLO by 2–3x during worst 30s.
   - If spike is sustained >3min, autoscaler will add replica sets and latency returns to baseline.
5) Streaming-specific notes:
   - First-token latency: warm instances meet <120ms; cold path can push first-token to 300–600ms.
   - For progressive UX we recommend client-side optimistic rendering with graceful rejoin on late first-token.

Action items (owner in parentheses):
- I will produce a trimmed runbook PDF for your SREs (Aditya) — attached.
- Ben to share an example Slack playbook and a sample circuit-breaker config we use with customers (Ben).
- Marissa to propose a short capacity add-on quote for Dedicated warm-pool sizing (Marissa).

Can you confirm the model family (7B/13B/30B) you plan to use in production and the percent streaming sessions vs batch inference? That will let us finalize warm-pool numbers.

Thanks — Aditya
Aditya Rao
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Claire Zhou <c.zhou@osprey-ai.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2029-04-11T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: warm-pool gating + queueing behavior (post-demo)

Adding a few operational examples from other customers to give you a feel for real behavior. Quick notes: 

Example A (retail flash-sale): 3k -> 6k spike for 90s, used a 10-instance warm-pool + 30s aggressive seeding. Results: median p95 stayed within target; p99 spiked for 20s during kernel init.

Example B (chat-first product): 1.2k baseline, 4k 60s spike, streaming-heavy: required client-side 2s optimistic window + 25% extra warm capacity to keep first-token under 150ms for >95% sessions.

Slack playbook snippet (paste-ready):
- Alert: \"WarmPoolPressure > 80%\" -> channel #ops-alerts + runbook link
- If queue depth > 1500 : increase warm-pool by 25% (manual toggle for first 2 events)
- If average first-token latency > 250ms for 5m: escalate to SRE oncall

I'll attach a short config example in Slack format to the shared Drive.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Claire Zhou <c.zhou@osprey-ai.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2029-04-11T14:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: warm-pool gating + queueing behavior (post-demo)

Thanks — this is exactly what we needed. Quick answers to your questions:
- Model family: predominantly 13B for production; we have a small % (10%) of 30B for high-fidelity flows.
- Streaming vs batch: ~70% streaming chat sessions, 30% embedding/batch calls.

Next steps from Osprey:
- We'll provision an initial warm-pool of 12 medium instances in us-west, as you suggested.
- Ops will run the attached trace against the warmup_runbook to validate queuing behavior and report p95/p99 to you by EOD Friday.
- We'll adopt the circuit-breaker suggestion at 30s and test UX fallback.

Could you also include the minimal telemetry keys we should send to your observability hooks so you can map warm-pool pressure and queue depth into the Redwood Console dashboards?

Claire
—Claire Zhou
Head of Infrastructure
Osprey AI

title: Delayed Supplier Invite Delivery due to DMARC Quarantine - Cypressware
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira.k@cypressware.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Julien Moreau <julien@cypressware.com>
Date: Sun, 7 Apr 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Supplier portal invite not arriving / quarantined by Google

Hi Ben,

We were expecting the supplier portal invite this morning but none of our finance team members received it. Our security team pulled the delivery logs and it looks like messages from your invites domain are being quarantined by Gmail/Workspace under DMARC (quarantine). We can see a header rewrite and the message is flagged as: "Authentication-Results: dmarc=quarantine (policy=quarantine)".

I've attached a screenshot of the Gmail quarantine notice and an anonymized copy of the SMTP headers. Can you either resend from a different sender or provide a manual activation link that we can use? We're trying to get purchase order (PO) setup started — vendor onboarding is blocking our first PO for this quarter.

Also, is there any special PO metadata you need from us (tax id, remit address, W9)? Julien can attach documents once we have a working invite method.

Thanks,
Samira
Samira Khan
Vendor Ops, Cypressware


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@cypressware.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood-inference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Sun, 7 Apr 2029 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Supplier portal invite not arriving / quarantined by Google

Hi Samira — thanks for the quick note and for attaching the headers.

Sorry about the friction. We see intermittent carrier-level DMARC/ARC breadcrumbing that can trigger Gmail's quarantine for certain provider rewriting rules. Two immediate options:

- I can generate a one-time manual activation URL (expires in 48h) and send it to an alternate address you control (e.g., a non-corporate alias) so your team can claim the account and finish onboarding.
- Our ops team can resend invites from support@redwood-inference.com (different envelope/from) which has slightly different DKIM/SPF alignment and has worked around quarantines in the past.

I'll loop in Rachel (on Compliance & Onboarding) and Karthik (technical) to share logs and confirm the best path — also to capture PO metadata requirements so you can attach the W9 and remit info. Date: 2029-03-10 11:35:20 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial vendor docs for NovaPulse — W-9 attached

Hi Julia — thanks for sending this over and welcome. I uploaded your W-9 into our procurement portal but the automated OCR flagged a name/EIN mismatch on the first page (screenshot attached).

Short version: the portal extracted the EIN as 12-3456789 but the scanned legal name came through as "NovaPulse LLC" while the W-9 lists "NovaPulse, Inc." — that triggers our procurement hold until we can confirm the legal entity matches payment records.

Can you confirm the legal entity (DBA vs legal) and either: 1) re-upload a higher-resolution scan (preferred), 2) provide a bank letter showing the EIN, or 3) authorize a signed notary letter? DocuSign is acceptable for the authorization if that is easier.

If you can get something to me by tomorrow morning I can push for a provisional PO release while finance finishes the manual verification.

Ben
Ben Carter | Vendor Onboarding Specialist\Redwood Inference\ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0191
Attachment: w9-ocr-flag-screenshot.png (image/png, 86 KB)

Cc: onboarding@novapulse.com

Subject: Re: Initial vendor docs for NovaPulse — W-9 attached

Hi Ben — thanks for the heads up and sorry about the scan quality. We ran a quick check with our finance team and can provide either a signed bank letter (on bank letterhead with EIN) or a notarized statement. DocuSign is fine for signatures but the bank letter will need to be an original on letterhead (we can provide a PDF sent directly from the bank if that helps).

I've attached a cleaner scan (novapulse_w9_cleaned_scan.pdf) — if that clears the OCR we can avoid the bank letter. If not, we'll request the bank to generate the letter this morning and route it to you.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — Julia

--

Hi Samantha / Diego —

- introduce the delivery team and roles,
- confirm scope for week 0 / week 1 tasks (SSO, sample traffic, eval prompts), and
- lock down the recurring cadence for the implementation sprint.

- Tue Jan 16 — 10:00–11:00
- Wed Jan 17 — 14:00–15:00
- Thu Jan 18 — 09:00–10:00

title: GammaEdge capacity readiness and soft-hold request
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 30 Nov 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: GammaEdge — GPU sprint availability check

Team,

GammaEdge (late-stage, deal ID DEAL-8721-GAMMA) just signaled a target kickoff around Dec 15 for their production pilot. Sales asked me to confirm whether we can support a dedicated sprint for the first 60 days with the following sizing ask: sustained throughput equivalent to ~40 A100-80GB devices (they will accept a mixture of H100 and A100-equivalents if we provide clear autoscaling).

Questions:
- Can infra commit a soft hold on the necessary capacity (US region) for a 30–45 day window while contract negotiations finish?
- What’s the realistic node mix we can reserve (H100 vs A100 vs quantized 4-bit alternatives)?
- Any scheduling constraints (maintenance windows, slot+slice limitations) that would block a mid-Dec start?

Please reply with a short availability snapshot and any immediate blockers. If we can do a provisional reservation, call out the steps so I can coordinate with the customer (they may sign an MOU/standby fee quickly).

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0123

---
Attachments: none in this message


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 30 Nov 2028 12:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: GammaEdge — GPU sprint availability check

Ben — quick ops snapshot below (preliminary; attaching detailed CSV):

Summary (US-west primary region):
- Current pool free: ~48 A100-equivalent units available across two pools.
- H100 inventory: limited; can guarantee 8 H100 nodes (sustained) but must avoid scheduling conflicts with existing reserved clusters.
- Quantized (4-bit) path: we can reduce effective footprint by ~25–35% for most of their inference load depending on model family.
- Recommendation: soft-hold 32 A100-equivalent units now; escalate to reserve an additional 16 units (or 8 H100) subject to confirmed MOU.

Constraints/Notes:
- Maintenance blackout windows: Dec 21–23 for several racks; we should avoid scheduling heavy resizes then.
- We need a formal ops ticket to convert soft-hold into a hard reservation (ticketing will lock nodes).

Next steps I can take if you want:
1) Place provisional soft hold (non-billable) for 14 days while legal/finance review.
2) Open CAP ticket to block nodes pending signed MOU (requires approval from Capacity Ops lead).

Attaching: fleet-availability-2028-11-30.csv (CSV: pool, node-type, free-units, maintenance-windows) and capacity-map-us-west.png

—Monica
Monica Patel
Capacity Planning Lead | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: fleet-availability-2028-11-30.csv, capacity-map-us-west.png

Priya — quick status and asks from our side so we can align on a provisional reservation: 

Summary from ops: we can provision a soft hold in US-west for ~32 A100-equivalent units immediately and escalate to reserve additional capacity (8 H100 or equivalent) once we have a signed mutual action plan. There are maintenance blackout dates Dec 21–23 to avoid.

To proceed we need from GammaEdge:
- Confirmation of expected peak and sustained qps (ballpark): is the 40 A100-equivalent target peak or sustained?
- Whether GammaEdge is willing to sign a 30–45 day provisional MOU/standby fee to lock additional H100 capacity if needed. DocuSign link below is for an MOU template we typically use for provisional holds.

DocuSign (provisional MOU): https://www.docusign.com/packet/ZX-9921-GB
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/8721

If GammaEdge confirms, Monica will place a 14-day soft hold, and we can convert to a hard CAP ticket once legal signs the MOU. Let me know and I’ll coordinate SOW + procurement next steps.

Best,
Ben

Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@gammaedge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@gammaedge.ai
Date: Fri, 01 Dec 2028 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: GammaEdge capacity readiness and soft-hold request

Ben,

Thanks for the clarity. Delta on our side: the 40 A100-equivalent number is peak expected for batch reranks; sustained inference footprint should average closer to 22–28 A100-equivalents. We likely need headroom for bursts to 40 for ~10% of traffic.

We are prepared to sign the provisional MOU and cover a reasonable standby commitment to secure H100 headroom for the initial 60 days. Please place the soft hold and send the MOU; procurement will route quickly. Attaching a draft SOW (gammaedge-sow-draft-20281201.pdf) with the high-level timelines.

Regards,
Priya Sharma
Head of Platform | GammaEdge
priya.sharma@gammaedge.ai

Attachments: gammaedge-sow-draft-20281201.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 01 Dec 2028 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: GammaEdge capacity readiness and soft-hold request

Ben/Monica — technical ops recommendation:

- Given GammaEdge’s burst/sustained profile (peak 40 / sustained ~25), we should:
  * Soft-hold 32 A100-equivalents in US-west now.
  * Prepare to convert 8 of those units to H100-backed nodes on confirmation (they agreed to standby fee).
- To reduce peak footprint: enable mixed precision + 4-bit quantization for their model family (if they accept a modest accuracy delta), which buys ~30% headroom and reduces required hard reservation.
- Zoning: split the reservation across two pools (primary pool + hot-spare pool) to provide fast failover without hitting maintenance windows.

Operational asks:
1) Capacity Ops: issue CAP ticket and block nodes (soft => CAP placeholder; hard => attachment of signed MOU).
2) Sales: confirm MOU cadence and standby fees (Ben).

If you want, I can draft the exact CAP ticket body and list of node IDs for reservation.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal Infra Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com

Ben

title: Short window service patch — immediate verification requested
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-11-14T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Short window patch required for scheduler jitter (customer-facing note drafted)

Team —

We found a narrow but reproducible regression in the request scheduler that intermittently increases tail latency when clients send many concurrent short streaming requests (multi-token streaming bursts). Root cause: race in the slot reclamation path under high concurrent interleaving. Impact is limited but visible on a subset of customers (notably Acme Retail) as 200–400ms spikes on tail p50->p99 for short requests.

Planned immediate mitigation: a lightweight hotfix to force a deterministic slot release path and tighten the reclamation lock window. This is a short, in-process patch (no model reload) that we can deploy cluster-by-cluster with a 30–40 minute maintenance window. Expect transient reconnects for long-lived server-sent streams during the individual node restart; regular REST calls will get routed to healthy nodes and face at most a 1–2s retry.

Customer-facing note below (draft) — please review and sign off: we need Ben to approve the tone and Laura to prepare the verification checklist and post-patch logs link. Priyom, please own the rollout (canary -> region).

Attachment: patch runbook (hotfix_patch_20281114.diff) and verification_checklist.txt

Thanks,
Aditya
Director, Platform Reliability
Redwood Inference

---
Draft customer-facing message (internal):
Subject: Short maintenance window today — brief patch to improve streaming latency

Hi Sabrina —

We’re reaching out to let you know we’ve identified a short scheduler issue that can cause occasional spikes in streaming latency. We’re planning a short hotfix today (target start 09:30 PST) to address this. The fix is low-risk, will not change model versions, and we expect minimal disruption. You may see transient reconnects for long streaming sessions during node restarts; REST calls should continue to be served with automatic retries.

We’ll confirm completion and provide a brief checklist so you can verify your endpoints. If you’d prefer we schedule a different time, let me know immediately.

-Aditya


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Sabrina Mills <sabrina@acmeretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-11-14T08:45:00-08:00
Subject: Short maintenance window today — brief patch to improve streaming latency

Sabrina —

Thanks for the quick sync earlier this week. I wanted to give you a heads-up that we’re deploying a short hotfix for a scheduler race that can cause intermittent tail-latency spikes for streaming-heavy workloads.

Planned start: 09:30 PST
Estimated duration: 30–40 minutes (per cluster node, rolling)
Risk: low — no model reloads, no customer data movement. There will be node restarts; long-lived streaming sessions may reconnect. REST requests will be routed to healthy nodes and may be retried automatically.

What we need from you:
- Please have one member of your ops team on standby at the start time to confirm endpoint health.
- After we confirm completion, please run the short verification checklist we attached (verification_checklist.txt) and reply with any anomalies.

We’ll post real-time updates to our status page during the rollout: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/20281114-scheduler

Attachments: hotfix_patch_20281114.diff (runbook excerpt), verification_checklist.txt

If you have constraints that prevent us from running at 09:30 PST, tell me ASAP and we’ll move the window. Otherwise we’ll proceed.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Director, Platform Reliability
Redwood Inference

1) Will session tokens or in-flight conversational state be invalidated for customers during the node restart? We want to know if we should expect client-side re-auth or reinitialization.
2) Does the verification checklist include a smoke test for streaming continuity and token validity?

If both are a no-impact (or minimal automatic retry), we’re fine with the window.

– Sabrina

Acme Retail

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Sabrina Mills <sabrina@acmeretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-11-14T11:05:00-08:00
Subject: Patch completed — short rolling hotfix applied (verification & notes)

Sabrina / Acme Ops —

Summary: The rolling hotfix to the scheduler slot reclamation path completed at 10:50 PST for Acme’s region. We deployed cluster-by-cluster with minimal impact; there were a small number of streaming reconnections (expected) and no REST request failures beyond automatic retries. No session tokens were invalidated and we did not evict any KV cache entries.

Actions we took:
- Applied deterministic slot-release patch to all nodes in the region.
- Monitored tail-latency (p50/p95/p99) and load; observed p99 drop from ~420ms to ~110–140ms for short streaming traces.
- Collected post-patch logs and a snapshot of request traces; link: https://drive.redwood.ai/shared/patch-runbook-2028-11-14 (read-only).

What we recommend you verify:
1) Run the attached verification_checklist.txt steps (quick smoke tests for streaming and REST endpoints).
2) Confirm that any long-lived sessions you care about re-established successfully (they should; reconnects are automatic).
3) If you see any auth errors, please capture a request-id and time and paste it into the reply — we’ll pull full traces.

If everything looks normal on your side we’ll close the incident in the status page. If you’d like a short postmortem summary and timeline for your records, I can circulate one by EOD tomorrow.

Thanks for the patience and the quick coordination.

Aditya Rao
Director, Platform Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments: verification_checklist.txt

title: NOVAtech: sustained inference slowdowns after canary - timeline & ask
preview:
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@novatech.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: NOVA - sudden increase in latency and errors since last canary

Alex —

We started seeing a significant degradation in inference performance across several endpoints shortly after your team flipped the canary yesterday afternoon. Our P95 jumped from ~110ms to ~480ms and P99s are consistently 1.4–1.9s. Error rates climbed from 0.2% to ~3% during heavy tails. This is impacting synchronous search flows; downstream timeouts have triggered customer complaints.

We rolled back our own client-side retries but the issue tracks to your hosted endpoints (region us-west2). The spike aligns with the canary timestamp: Nov 9 14:40 PST. Attached are a small sample of traces and our aggregated latency metrics.

We need an immediate mitigation plan and timeline for a rollback or other fix. SLA credits and customer communication will likely be required if this persists. Please advise next steps ASAP — our production traffic has retries and queuing that are beginning to cascade.

Regards,
Sanjay Rao
Head of Platform Engineering
NOVAtech

attachments: novatech_traces_sample.txt, novatech_latency_logs_20281109.zip

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@novatech.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 15:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: NOVA - sudden increase in latency and errors since last canary

Sanjay — thanks for the quick signal and the traces, this is top priority. I'm escalating to SRE/serving and will coordinate a mitigation plan now.

Immediate actions we are starting:
- Marissa and team will validate whether the canary routing flag fully propagated and capture in-flight traffic (we'll begin a capture window now).
- If we see a regression tied to the variant, we'll flip routing back to the stable model within a 15–30 minute window.
- I will request a short SLA credit while we validate root cause; we'll confirm the amount after we quantify impacted qps.

Can you confirm: (1) whether you still see the same issue in us-east; (2) if you have a preferred rollback window (we can do immediate rollback but want to coordinate to avoid client-side flapping)?

I'll keep you updated every 15 mins until we stabilize.

Thanks — Alex
Alex Martinez | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
T: +1 415 555 0198

On Thu, Nov 9, 2028 at 3:12 PM Sanjay Rao <sanjay@novatech.ai> wrote:
> Alex —
> ... (original message)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 15:41:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: NOVA - sudden increase in latency and errors since last canary

Alex/Ben — I'm live on this. Quick summary from an SRE perspective after an initial pass on the captures:
- Canary variant exposes a slightly different attention kernel and has higher KV cache churn on longer prompt workloads. We see a ~22% slower per-token path when the model hits long context (800+ tokens).
- Routing shows ~12% of NOVA traffic was routed to the canary in us-west2 (matches their window).
- There is an interaction with our prefix caching: large short-lived prefixes caused cache eviction that increases cold-path QKV compute.

Immediate remediation options:
1) Toggle routing to stable variant for NOVA (model flag flip) — fastest, low-risk; will require ~5–10s propagation and minor extra qps on stable.
2) Apply a temporary routing weight reduction (e.g., 100% -> 5%) while we apply a targeted micro-patch to the canary kernel.
3) If they prefer, we can force a selective degrader that routes long-context requests to a smaller quantized fallback model.

I attached the canary-config-diff that highlights the kernel and kv-cache defaults. If Alex approves, I'll push route-toggle and monitor.

Marissa Cole
SRE — Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

attachments: canary-config-diff.txt

Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 15:47:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: NOVA - sudden increase in latency and errors since last canary

Sanjay — appreciate the signal and the traces. NOVA is a high-value account and we want to minimize customer impact. Alex: please proceed with Marissa's recommended route-toggle to stable (option 1) and let's communicate a rollback window of immediate -> +15 mins to NOVA.

Grace — FYI, we will need to discuss a goodwill credit and a short customer note. I'm proposing a 7% usage credit for the affected window, pending exact qps analysis. If leadership wants to standardize the credit or cap it, let me know.

Alex — confirm and proceed; I'll draft the customer note and coordinate CS outreach.

— Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 16:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Exec brief: NOVA slowdown — immediate mitigation & asks

Jonas/Leadership — short brief. NOVA (DEAL-4478) reported a production degradation synchronous with our canary flip at 14:40 PST. Impact: P95 from ~110ms -> ~480ms; P99s up to ~1.9s; error rate delta ~+2.8% for a 20-minute window.

What we did (15:28–16:02 PST):
- Marissa identified a kernel + cache interaction in the canary variant.
- We toggled routing back to stable for NOVA at 15:48 PST. Propagation completed by 15:55 PST; metrics started trending back to baseline.
- We captured traces, config diffs and started a detailed postmortem. Attachments are in the incident link.

Asks:
- Approve a provisional 7% usage credit for the impacted window; we'll reconcile after exact qps math.
- Approve prioritization of a micro-patch on the canary kernel to address KV cache behavior (target: hotfix in 48 hours).
- Allow CS to send a brief customer-facing incident note (I will draft).

Alex Martinez

title: Investigating persistent tail-latency causing duplicate order writes — escalation
preview:
From: Kelsey Morris <kelsey@fastretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: ops+alerts@fastretail.com
Date: Fri, 03 Nov 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Strange behavior: duplicate orders and long response tails

Hi Ben,

We started seeing a small number of duplicate order records yesterday evening (around 20 occurrences across several checkout endpoints). Each duplicate correlates with a long response time from Redwood — in the audit logs the create-order API call shows a 10–50s response in the tail. The customer-facing symptom is double charges and duplicate order confirmations.

Can Redwood take a look and confirm whether this is an SDK retry pattern or something server-side (we're on the hosted API). We have a high-priority incident with financing and ops.

Kelsey
Head of Platform Ops, FastRetail

---
Attachment: order-sample-11-02.csv (csv)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Kelsey Morris <kelsey@fastretail.com>, ops+alerts@fastretail.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 03 Nov 2028 09:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Strange behavior: duplicate orders and long response tails

Kelsey — thanks for the heads up. I pulled the initial request traces you attached. Early hypothesis: long-tail latency is coming from intermittent queueing/backpressure in the tenant routing layer for FastRetail (their traffic profile has heavy bursts around batch job windows). When request latency spikes we’ve seen SDKs retry; combined with at-least-once handler semantics in their integration the retries look like duplicate writes.

I’m opening an internal investigation now. Lauren/Marissa — can you validate whether the tenant has any recent routing overrides or a changed concurrency cap? Also, can infra share any KV cache saturation or de-dup token errors for FR-ENT-2345 in the 11/2–11/3 window?

Immediate asks:
- Can infra dump kvcache metrics and per-shard queue length for FR-ENT-2345 (last 72h)
- Support to run a temporary routing rule that pins FastRetail to a slightly higher concurrency cap until we have a safe mitigation
- Product to prepare a concise customer-facing summary and proposed timeline for resolution (we should avoid mentioning internal quota names)

Ben
Senior AE, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Kelsey Morris <kelsey@fastretail.com>
Date: Fri, 03 Nov 2028 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Strange behavior: duplicate orders and long response tails

Ben — I’m on it. Initial check: no recent manual routing overrides. I see several short-lived p99 spikes tied to busy windows on Nov 2 around 02:00 UTC — those line up with FastRetail batch flushes. I’ve requested the infra CSV export for kvcache and queueing (should arrive in ~20m).

For customer comms: recommend we propose two immediate mitigations to Kelsey — (A) temporary routing to a dedicated pool for critical checkout endpoints for 48–72 hours, or (B) enable optimistic de-dup token enforcement in their app (if FastRetail can accept minimal client changes, not ideal mid-incident). I’ll draft the 1-par exec note after we have the infra dump.

-Marissa
Director, Customer Success


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Kelsey Morris <kelsey@fastretail.com>, Compliance Team <security@fastretail.com>
Date: Sun, 05 Nov 2028 14:38:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Strange behavior: duplicate orders and long response tails

Team — posting the initial infra findings. Attached: kvcache-metrics-11-04.csv and trace bundle (trace-20281105.zip). Highlights:
- Multiple kvcache miss storms on 11/02 01:48–02:30 UTC coincident with FastRetail batch window; shard-level queue length briefly exceeded the autoscale threshold, which caused backpressure and request queueing.
- Concurrency cap for FR-ENT-2345 was at 40 (configured) — during the spill we saw bursts that effectively saturated the shard slots. Autoscaler did not react quickly enough because the burst pattern was short and CPU headroom was limited due to co-located tenants.
- Several request traces show retries from the client SDK after 10s without an idempotency token set on the application side. The combo of queueing and client retry produced duplicate write traces.

Proposed short-term mitigation (approved by infra on call):
1) Temporary emergency routing: pin critical checkout routes to a dedicated small pool for 72 hours (we can allocate from burst reserve).
2) Increase concurrency cap for FR-ENT-2345 from 40 -> 80 for 24 hours and monitor p99.
3) Apply a soft-rate-limit for upstream SDK calls and return a 429 with Retry-After for non-critical endpoints to prevent noisy retries.

I’ve prepared the attached runbook-temp-routing.pdf for ops to implement step 1. We need a C-level sign-off if we move to dedicated pool beyond 72 hours (cost bump). Marissa — I’ll need you to run the customer message.

-Lauren
VP, Solutions Engineering

---
Attachments: trace-20281105.zip (zip), kvcache-metrics-11-04.csv (csv), runbook-temp-routing.pdf (pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Kelsey Morris <kelsey@fastretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 05 Nov 2028 18:04:00 -0800
Subject: Action plan and next steps — duplicate orders investigation

Kelsey — thanks for your patience. Quick summary and action items for FastRetail:

What happened (summary):
- During your nightly batch window on 11/02 we observed short-lived kvcache miss storms that briefly saturated queue slots in the serving layer. This caused long-tail request latency (10–50s) for a subset of requests. Some of your clients retried the request without idempotency tokens which led to duplicate order writes.

What we are doing now (immediate mitigations):
- We will pin your checkout routes to a dedicated small pool for 72 hours starting 2028-11-06 00:00 UTC to eliminate cross-tenant interference.
- Increase concurrency cap to 80 for 24 hours starting same time; we will revert unless you request extension.
- We’ll enable a soft 429 on noisy non-critical endpoints to reduce retry storms.

Customer asks for you:
- If possible, deploy an idempotency token (de-dup key) for checkout within the next 72 hours — this is the long-term fix.
- Please share any SDK-side retry policies that could be tuned.

We will monitor and send hourly updates during the first 12 hours after the change. If the above solves the issue, we’ll coordinate a postmortem and any credits separately.

Ben
Senior AE, Redwood Inference


From: Kelsey Morris <kelsey@fastretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Compliance Team <security@fastretail.com>
Date: Tue, 08 Nov 2028 17:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Action plan and next steps — duplicate orders investigation

Ben / team — thank you. We accepted the temporary routing change and are seeing p99 drop significantly in the first 3 hours. Ops confirms duplicate incidents have stopped for new traffic. We’re scanning the backlog and will follow up on the postmortem items (idempotency token rollout is on a sprint for next week).

Compliance flagged that they need the runbook and trace bundle for audit; I’ve shared your attachments with them and they asked for the Jira ticket for tracking. Please confirm we can keep the dedicated routing in place until end of day 11/09 while we finish the backlog scan.

Kelsey

---

title: Post-demo model fit notes, benchmark links, and proposed safety/pin plan
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena.cruz@novacorp.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 2 Nov 2028 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Post-demo model fit notes, benchmark links, and proposed safety/pin plan

Hi Elena — thanks again for the demo time today. Quick recap and recommended next steps following our walkthrough of the search & summary flows: 

Summary of fit notes
- Primary signal: your relevance precision and short-summary coherence looked strong on the first-pass prompts we ran in the demo.
- Red flags: We observed edge-case hallucination on SKU-level attributes (examples attached) and some sensitivity to punctuation in user queries that affects recall.

Recommended model variants for pilot
- redwood/llama-3-13b-v1.4 (stable; current production snapshot) — highest fidelity on Novacorp golden set
- redwood/optimus-7b-fp16 (lower cost, slightly lower recall; good for non-critical bulk inference)

Benchmarks & artifacts
- Prompt set used in demo: attached (novacorp_prompt_set_v1.json)
- Benchmark summary and full metric export: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1aBcD_example_benchmarks
- Ops ticket (opened for monitoring + regression tracking): https://jira.redwood.com/browse/OPS-4521

Quality-regression concern and proposed safeguard
- You mentioned you recently saw output quality decline after a provider-side model refresh. If we detect a regression on your golden set vs baseline, we recommend an immediate pin to the last-known-good model snapshot (llama-3-13b-v1.4) and a canary rollout to the newer variant with a 10% traffic guard.

Action items (proposed)
1) Confirm whether you want us to pin all traffic to v1.4 while we triage (I can request the pin immediately).
2) Share any additional failing examples (we included what you provided in regression_examples_20281102.jsonl).
3) If you’re comfortable, we’ll spin up a 10% canary to v1.5 with strict length/time limits and snapshot logging.

I’ve attached the prompt set and a short benchmark PDF. Rishi will follow up with a brief analysis of the delta if you decide to pin/rollback.

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call if that’s easier.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123
Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: novacorp_prompt_set_v1.json (prompt set, JSON), benchmark_summary_v1.pdf (summary PDF)

From: Elena Cruz <elena.cruz@novacorp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 2 Nov 2028 16:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-demo model fit notes, benchmark links, and proposed safety/pin plan

Thanks Ava — appreciate the clear recap. A couple of urgent points from our side: 

1) We shipped a minor model update last week (vendor-deployed patch) and since then we’ve seen a ~6% drop in SKU-level attribute recall in production for two of our higher-value customers. I pasted two examples below where the new model replaces a specific SKU with an unrelated product name (these were fine in last week’s export).

Example 1: input: \"Find 12oz cold-brew, SKU 44321\"
Expected: returns SKU 44321 metadata
Now: returns \"cold-brew mix 12oz\" with different product id

Example 2: input: \"Which charger fits model X-200?\"
Expected: lists compatible chargers
Now: returns a generic recommendation unrelated to X-200

2) We need a timeline for rollback/pin. Our procurement/legal teams are watching this because it affects upcoming renewals. We’d prefer an immediate pin to the prior snapshot until you can confirm stability.

Can you confirm whether pinning is an account-level change or per-route? Also need an estimate for how long the pin would take to activate and any visibility we’ll get into logs/latency differences.

Elena Cruz
Head of Product — Novacorp Retail Systems
elena.cruz@novacorp.com

> Hi Elena — thanks again for the demo time today. Quick recap and recommended next steps following our walkthrough of the search & summary flows: ...


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Elena Cruz <elena.cruz@novacorp.com>
Date: Thu, 2 Nov 2028 17:01:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-demo model fit notes, benchmark links, and proposed safety/pin plan

Hi all — quick technical summary and concrete plan from the infra/ops side. I’ve included Elena on this note to keep you updated on steps and timelines.

Technical snapshot
- Change: the v1.5 refresh included a tokenizer normalization tweak and a small prompt-space temperature adjustment intended to reduce hallucination in long-form.
- Observed impact: on Novacorp golden set (N=1,200) we measured a 5.2% absolute drop in SKU recall and a small increase (+0.6%) in diversity. The attached file regression_examples_20281102.jsonl has the failing examples you sent plus machine diffs.

Immediate mitigation options (recommended priority)
A) Account-level pin to llama-3-13b-v1.4 (recommended) — effect: all routes will serve v1.4; activation target: ~15 minutes after approval; visibility: we’ll publish a pin activation note and environment id in OPS-4521.
B) Per-route pin (if you prefer selectivity) — effect: we can pin only /search and /sku-resolution routes; activation target: ~10–20 minutes per route, depending on active connections.
C) Canary (safety-forward): deploy v1.5 to 10% of calls with logging + golden-set latency/quality alerts. If regression persists above thresholds we auto-failover to pinned snapshot.

Recommended path: Approve option A for immediate customer safety, then run option C to validate v1.5 with strict rollback triggers (threshold: >2% drop in SKU recall on golden set over 1h).

Operational notes
- Rollback window: we can pin back to v1.4 indefinitely; rolling forward from a pin requires explicit change control (owner sign-off).
- Metrics & visibility: we’ll grant Novacorp a read-only dashboard for token-level cost and per-route quality traces within 30 minutes of pin activation.
- Timeline: once Ava confirms, I’ll request the pin and attach OPS-4521 updates; ETA to show pinned traffic: ~15 minutes.

Next steps for this thread
1) Ava — confirm whether Novacorp wants account-level or per-route pin.
2) Elena — if you approve pin, I’ll execute and report back once pinned.
3) Marissa — please prepare a short customer-facing note that explains the pin and next steps for procurement/legal.

Rishi Malhotra
Senior Site Reliability Engineer — Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.com

---
Attachments: regression_examples_20281102.jsonl (examples + diffs)

From: Elena Cruz <elena.cruz@novacorp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Novacorp Procurement <procurement@novacorp.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 2 Nov 2028 17:35:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-demo model fit notes, benchmark links, and proposed safety/pin plan

Thanks Rishi — let’s proceed with option A (account-level pin to llama-3-13b-v1.4) immediately. We’ll accept a 10% canary to v1.5 after the pin is active and after you’ve enabled snapshot logging for the golden set.

Please include procurement on timing/impact notes and any credit/compensation proposals; we’ll escalate internally if the pin isn’t active within 60 minutes.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Elena

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>

Hi all — quick technical summary and concrete plan from the infra/ops side. I’ve included Elena on this note to keep you updated on steps and timelines.

Technical snapshot
- Change: the v1.5 refresh included a tokenizer normalization tweak and a small prompt-space temperature adjustment intended to reduce hallucination in long-form.
- Observed impact: on Novacorp golden set (N=1,200) we measured a 5.2% absolute drop in SKU recall and a small increase (+0.6%) in diversity. The attached file regression_examples_20281102.jsonl has the failing examples you sent plus machine diffs.

Immediate mitigation options (recommended priority)
A) Account-level pin to llama-3-13b-v1.4 (recommended) — effect: all routes will serve v1.4; activation target: ~15 minutes after approval; visibility: we’ll publish a pin activation note and environment id in OPS-4521.
B) Per-route pin (if you prefer selectivity) — effect: we can pin only /search and /sku-resolution routes; activation target: ~10–20 minutes per route, depending on active connections.
C) Canary (safety-forward): deploy v1.5 to 10% of calls with logging + golden-set latency/quality alerts. If regression persists above thresholds we auto-failover to pinned snapshot.

Recommended path: Approve option A for immediate customer safety, then run option C to validate v1.5 with strict rollback triggers (threshold: >2% drop in SKU recall on golden set over 1h).

Operational notes
- Rollback window: we can pin back to v1.4 indefinitely; rolling forward from a pin requires explicit change control (owner sign-off).
- Metrics & visibility: we’ll grant Novacorp a read-only dashboard for token-level cost and per-route quality traces within 30 minutes of pin activation.
- Timeline: once Ava confirms, I’ll request the pin and attach OPS-4521 updates; ETA to show pinned traffic: ~15 minutes.

To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-10-20T08:15:00-07:00
Subject: Request: retention/location mapping + proof-of-wipe for DPA

Hi Camila,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. As part of our DPA review we'd like a clear mapping of how Redwood classifies data categories (PII, PHI, telemetry, embeddings, logs) to retention windows and physical location (onshore/single-tenant vs multi-region). Specifically we need:

- A mapping table that shows for each data category: primary storage location, replication regions, backup snapshot retention, and typical deletion/wipe behavior (including if/when snapshots are overwritten).
- The type of evidence you can provide to prove deletion/wipe (e.g., snapshot manifests, KMS key rotation logs, deletion job IDs, signed attestation).
- Whether we can carve out a subset labeled "Aurum PII" to be kept onshore only and have a faster backup wipe window (30 days) vs standard (90+ days).
- Any operational constraints (maintenance windows, geo-fallbacks) that would affect onshore-only guarantees.

Can you also send a sample audit-log extract and a short runbook or checklist that shows the deletion workflow? Our legal and security teams want this before we sign the DPA addendum.

Looping in Legal and Procurement on our side. Happy to hop on a 30-min call if helpful.

Thanks,
Marta
Marta Alvarez
Head of Security Programs, Aurum Health
legal@aurumhealth.com

Hi Marta — thanks for the clear ask. We're happy to provide a mapping and evidence examples. Quick clarifying questions before we assemble the materials:

1) When you say "Aurum PII" can you confirm the attributes you want to include (e.g., name, DOB, SSN/identifier, medical record id)? A precise tag set will help us estimate onshore capacity and any impacts to cost.
2) Is the 30-day wipe a hard requirement for production + backups, or is that target for the primary copy only? Many customers accept a slightly longer backup retention for business continuity.
3) Do you require continuous onshore-only processing for inference, or just primary storage onshore with read replicas disabled outside the region?

I've attached a starter spreadsheet (retention-location-matrix-draft.xlsx) that maps our standard categories and retention defaults. Marcus and Marissa are looped in — Marcus will provide the backup/snapshot specifics and Marissa can speak to legal attestation language.

Proposed immediate next steps:
- We’ll update the matrix with an Aurum-specific column once you confirm the PII attributes.
- Marcus will compile a short runbook + sample audit extract.
- We can propose two contract options: (A) standard residency with 90-day backup retention + quarterly attestation, or (B) a compliance add-on with 30-day backup wipe and FORS (additional engineering + audit fee).

Would you be available Nov 1 for a quick walkthrough?

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com
Phone: +1 415-555-0138

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-10-23T11:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: retention/location mapping + proof-of-wipe for DPA (technical notes)

Camila / Marissa — adding technical details to feed into the matrix and the runbook. High level notes:

- Primary storage is customer-tenant logical separation; for hosted customers we maintain multi-region replication by default. We can enforce single-region primary storage for a labeled dataset (requires tagging at write time).
- Snapshot/backups: incremental snapshots taken daily; full snapshots rotated on a 90d cycle by default. Offsite disaster-recovery backups are kept 180d in cold storage for critical recovery.
- Deletion flow: a delete request marks the object for immediate logical deletion (removed from primary index), then a background compaction job removes content from active storage. Snapshots are immutable; we run a scheduled snapshot purge process that will remove snapshot manifests containing the object after the snapshot retention window.
- Evidence we can provide: snapshot manifest (hash + timestamp), KMS audit entry showing data key rotation/deletion event,scheduled purge job run id and timestamp, and a signed attestation on Redwood letterhead.
- Constraints: shortening snapshot retention to 30 days for backups is possible but requires a dedicated retention policy and an operational runbook (preferably with a paid professional services engagement). It also increases risk for some recovery scenarios.

Suggested defaults for Aurum PII carve-out:
- Primary storage: US-only region (single-region pin)
- Replication: disabled for non-prod; replication allowed with EU only as read-replica if explicitly authorized
- Backups: per-policy backup retention = 90 days (default), optional add-on to 30 days (requires PS and configuration lock)
- Cache/TTL: embedding cache TTL = 24h (can be reduced to ephemeral on demand)

I placed a short runbook and a sample audit-log extract in our internal Drive (link in doc we'll share to the customer). Happy to redact any sensitive internal ids before sending.

-Marcus
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance Lead, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com

--

Date: 2028-10-29T10:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: retention/location mapping + proof-of-wipe for DPA

Hi Marta — following up with what we can provide and a proposal for the DPA appendix. Key points below and attached artifacts: retention-location-matrix-v1.xlsx (customer-specific mapping) and attestation-sample.pdf (example of quarterly attestation).

What we can commit (baseline):
- We will tag an "Aurum PII" dataset at ingest to enforce single-region primary storage in the US.
- Primary deletion: logical delete on request; we will provide the delete job id and timestamp.
- Snapshots/backups: standard backup retention is 90 days; snapshot manifests are immutable until the retention period expires. We produce snapshot manifest files and can provide KMS audit events related to key usage.
- Evidence bundle: for each deletion event we can provide (a) snapshot manifest(s) showing object checksum and timestamps, (b) scheduled purge job id and timestamp when manifests are removed, (c) KMS audit log excerpts, (d) quarterly signed attestation.

Options to meet a shorter backup wipe window:
- Compliance Add-on (recommended): 30-day backup retention for Aurum PII. Requires a configuration lock, testing window, and professional services to validate the purge process. Estimated one-time fee + small recurring charge.
- Compromise: 60-day backup retention + monthly attestation (midpoint option).

Operational notes & SLA:
- We can provide quarterly attestation by default. For higher assurance (monthly) we can add an audit subscription.
- We are willing to include an appendix in the DPA that documents the mapping, evidence types, and the escalation path for discrepancy investigations.

Next steps we need from Aurum:
1) Finalize attribute list that defines "Aurum PII"
2) Confirm preferred retention option (90 / 60 / 30 with add-on)
3) Decide whether you want monthly or quarterly attestations

If you agree, I'll draft the DPA appendix language and Marissa will propose attestation wording. I can also send a DocuSign SOW for the compliance add-on if you want the 30-day option.

Best,
Camila Reyes

To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-10-23T11:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: retention/location mapping + proof-of-wipe for DPA (technical notes)

Camila / Marissa — adding technical details to feed into the matrix and the runbook. High level notes:

- Primary storage is customer-tenant logical separation; for hosted customers we maintain multi-region replication by default. We can enforce single-region primary storage for a labeled dataset (requires tagging at write time).
- Snapshot/backups: incremental snapshots taken daily; full snapshots rotated on a 90d cycle by default. Offsite disaster-recovery backups are kept 180d in cold storage for critical recovery.
- Deletion flow: a delete request marks the object for immediate logical deletion (removed from primary index), then a background compaction job removes content from active storage. Snapshots are immutable; we run a scheduled snapshot purge process that will remove snapshot manifests containing the object after the snapshot retention window.
- Evidence we can provide: snapshot manifest (hash + timestamp), KMS audit entry showing data key rotation/deletion event,scheduled purge job run id and timestamp, and a signed attestation on Redwood letterhead.
- Constraints: shortening snapshot retention to 30 days for backups is possible but requires a dedicated retention policy and an operational runbook (preferably with a paid professional services engagement). It also increases risk for some recovery scenarios.

Suggested defaults for Aurum PII carve-out:
- Primary storage: US-only region (single-region pin)
- Replication: disabled for non-prod; replication allowed with EU only as read-replica if explicitly authorized
- Backups: per-policy backup retention = 90 days (default), optional add-on to 30 days (requires PS and configuration lock)
- Cache/TTL: embedding cache TTL = 24h (can be reduced to ephemeral on demand)

I placed a short runbook and a sample audit-log extract in our internal Drive (link in doc we'll share to the customer). Happy to redact any sensitive internal ids before sending.

-Marcus
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance Lead, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com

Subject: Expansion request — enable three new project namespaces + capacity uplift

Hi Jonas,

Hope you had a great weekend. Quick ask from our product org: we want to activate three new project namespaces under our existing account (customer-facing chat, internal analytics, and billing-automation). Primary requests:
- Issue separate API keys scoped per project (key rotation to remain account-level)
- Staggered rollout over 3 weeks (start Nov 1)
- Increase committed qps from 150 -> 300 for the month of November to support burst testing

We're also hoping to align the term on those new projects with our annual contract (current renewal due Feb 1). Can you send a short proposal and expected delta in monthly committed spend?

Attaching a short doc with proposed timeline and functional owners.

Thanks Amit — happy to help. High-level response and next steps below.

Quick answers:
- We can provision three distinct API keys scoped per-project as requested. Keys will be issued with project prefixes (e.g. storwell-chat, storwell-analytics, storwell-billing).
- Staggered rollout starting Nov 1 is fine; propose one project enabled per week (Nov 1, Nov 8, Nov 15) to allow monitoring and throttling adjustments.
- To support burst testing, we can increase committed qps to 300 for November; we estimate the committed spend delta at roughly $18k for the month (detail in attached addendum).

Requests from our side:
1) Confirm if keys should be read-only for analytics or full access.
2) Provide a primary security contact for the key rotation plan.
3) Please confirm which three projects map to which internal owners (so we can set RBAC and dashboard access).

- We share finalized addendum and DocuSign link by Oct 20
- Key activation schedule triggered upon signed addendum

Subject: Re: Expansion request — enable three new project namespaces + capacity uplift

Jonas / Laura — quick ops and pricing notes (looping in customer so they have visibility):

title: Geographic guardrails evaluation & playbook — follow-up
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@novamed.com>, John Parker <j.parker@novamed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Oct 2028 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Geographic guardrails evaluation & playbook — follow-up

Hi Emma / John,

Thanks again for the time earlier — really useful to map NovaMed's flows and the strict PII/Risk zones you flagged. As promised, I pulled together a short recap and proposed next steps so we can qualify the residency constraints and show how Redwood would handle failover/backups without violating your data controls. Attached is a first-pass playbook and a slim data-map you can drop into the SOC/IT team.

Quick recap of what I heard on the call:
- Primary production region: EU (Frankfurt) for PHI and patient-identifiable records.
- Non-sensitive analytics/process flows: US region acceptable.
- APAC pilot: ingest in APAC region but must not replicate PHI out of APAC without explicit consent.
- Required controls: customer-managed KMS, per-tenant key separation, detailed request/response logging retained 180 days, and the ability to pin model serving to a region with warm-standby in another region for DR.

Open clarifying questions (need answers to qualify):
1) For the APAC pilot, can you confirm whether the data that flows through Redwood will ever include patient identifiers (names, MRNs, DOB) or will it be tokenized/hashed before sending?
2) For audit logs, do you require full request/response payload retention or truncated metadata + per-call hashes?
3) Do you expect cross-region failover to be automatic (redwood handles warm-standby activation) or manual with a runbook owned by NovaMed?
4) Any contractual requirements around RPO/RTO for backups (e.g., RPO <= 1 hour, RTO <= 4 hours)?

Proposed immediate next steps:
- You: drop the populated data-map (I attached a blank template) with examples of which fields are considered PHI.
- Us: we’ll produce an expanded playbook with pinning options (hard pin, soft affinity, and cost/latency tradeoffs) and a one-page table mapping controls (KMS, logging retention, encryption-at-rest/in-transit, SOC2 artifacts).
- Align on a short pilot plan for APAC ingestion that ensures no PHI leaves APAC during the pilot window.

Can you confirm who on your infra/compliance team we should include in the follow-up? I suggest a 30-minute technical validation next week after you fill the data-map.

Thanks — ava

--
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0112
redwoodinference.com

Attachments: redwood-geoguard-playbook-draft.pdf (PDF, 120KB), novamed-data-map.xlsx (Excel, 45KB)

From: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@novamed.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: John Parker <j.parker@novamed.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Oct 2028 16:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Geographic guardrails evaluation & playbook — follow-up

Thanks Ava — useful summary and appreciate the template.

Quick answers inline:
1) For APAC pilot: patient identifiers will NOT be sent raw. Our ingestion pipeline tokenizes MRNs and hashes names before any external transmission. We will attach a sample export to the data-map column.
2) Audit logs: Compliance requires retained truncated request/response payloads for 180 days plus a full cryptographic hash of the original payload. We cannot store full plaintext payloads in logs beyond 30 days.
3) Failover: initial preference is manual activation by our on-call infra lead (we want to validate runbooks). If the pilot succeeds we can explore automated warm-standby failover later.
4) RPO/RTO: target RPO is 1 hour, target RTO is 2 hours for production-critical endpoints.

I'm attaching the populated data-map and a short note from our security lead describing the KMS expectations (customer-managed keys, HSM-backed).

Emma

--
Emma Johnson
Head of IT Security, NovaMed Health
emma.johnson@novamed.com | +44 20 7946 0123

Attachments: novamed-data-map.xlsx (Excel, 65KB), novamed-kms-requirements.txt (TXT, 3KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@novamed.com>, John Parker <j.parker@novamed.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Oct 2028 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Geographic guardrails evaluation & playbook — follow-up

Great — thanks for the fast turnaround, Emma. Couple of operational notes for the playbook:
- We can support customer-managed KMS with per-tenant envelopes and HSM bindings; need the NovaMed KMS ARN and an initial test key to validate CMK rotation policies.
- Logging policy: will implement metadata + 256-bit SHA hash of the payload and store full ed25519-signed digests for 180 days. We will automatically redact fields marked as PHI in the data-map beyond 30 days unless you instruct otherwise.
- Failover model we recommend for your manual-first approach: maintain a warm-standby (replicated model weights and replicated KV caches) in EU and APAC so activation time stays under your 2-hour RTO target; warm-standby has modest incremental cost — we’ll include a rough cost delta in the playbook.

Action items for internal track:
- Ava: prepare the expanded playbook with three pinning modes and cost/latency table (ETA: 2028-10-07 EOD).
- Monica: schedule a 30-minute technical validation with NovaMed's infra and security (ETA: pick slots by Mon 2028-10-09).
- Ben: prepare an initial KMS integration checklist and the logging-retention config snippet.

I’ll also add an appendix describing how cross-region snapshot backups work (encrypted, customer-key-wrapped) and how we can do region-specific retention.

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@novamed.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Oct 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Geographic guardrails evaluation & playbook — follow-up

Looping in a short CTO-level note for contract/SLAs:
- If NovaMed requires customer-managed keys + per-tenant isolation + RPO <= 1 hour and RTO <= 2 hours, we should put together a Dedicated-residency addendum (Reserved capacity with pinned regions). That will change pricing to a committed-monthly model; I will draft a short addendum and send for legal review.
- For the pilot, we are fine to proceed with the manual failover approach and warm-standby replicas in EU/APAC; ensure we capture the runbook acceptance criteria in the playbook.

Next steps (external-facing):
1) Ava: share the expanded playbook (with pinning modes, cost table, KMS checklist) by EOD 2028-10-07 (per Ben).
2) NovaMed: confirm a 30-minute tech validation slot and share KMS ARNs/test key.
3) Internal: I’ll start a draft Dedicated addendum outlining commitments for region pinning and RPO/RTO.

Markus

--
Markus Klein
VP, Product Operations, Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwoodinference.com
