title: Wiring diagram for POC owners, legal gates & procurement milestones
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Rivers <ethan@carewell.ai>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@carewell.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Oct 2028 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap + wiring diagram proposal

Hi Ethan / Priya —

Thanks again for taking the demo this morning. Quick recap and a proposed "wiring diagram" to make the POC procurement path as predictable as possible. High-level notes from our side: 

- Success criteria we heard: (1) 99th pct latency < 500ms for chat endpoints; (2) automated logging of token-level usage for billing and audit; (3) data residency limited to US-east region.
- Suggested POC scope: 4-week technical trial with Redwood Dedicated (single GPU reservation), sample privacy-safe dataset, and two integration points: chat widget and backend batch scoring.

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@finos.com>, Julie Tan <julie@marketix.io>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Oct 2028 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap + request: VP/CTO handshake & technical deep dive windows

Hi Samir / Julie —

Thanks again for the time this morning. Quick recap and suggested next steps so we can keep momentum: 

- High level fit: Finos Analytics is looking to run a short pilot to evaluate inference cost and latency for your batch and interactive workloads (search rerank + chat-like summaries).
- Primary blockers you named: (1) executive alignment for procurement & SSO decisions, (2) a technical deep dive to validate latency profiles on your production-like sequences, and (3) a mutual action plan with success criteria for the pilot.

What I propose:
1) VP/CTO handshake (15–20 min) — introduce execs, confirm success criteria and procurement timeline.
2) Technical deep dive (45–60 min) — Connor to walk through runtime behavior, KV caching, and test harness we can run against a small sample.

To make scheduling easiest, can you confirm who from Finos would attend the executive handshake (VP of Engineering or CTO)? Once I have the attendee names I can propose two slots and loop in our CTO-equivalent contact if needed.

Proposed immediate next steps (attached draft MAP + SOW outline):
- Samir / Julie: confirm exec attendee(s) + up to three available 15–20 min windows in the next 7 business days.
- I’ll share two confirmed windows for the exec handshake and reserve a separate 60-min slot for the technical deep dive (Connor will run that).

Attachments: RWI-mutual-action-plan.xlsx (draft), SOW-outline.docx (high-level).

Thanks — looking forward to pushing this to a pilot kickoff.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
Calendly: https://calendly.com/ava-chen/30min

title: Targeted region surge — immediate traffic steering advice & recovery steps
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Acme AI <infra@acme.ai>, Morgan Chen <morgan@datasurge.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 02 Oct 2028 08:03:00 -0700
Subject: Targeted region surge — immediate traffic steering advice & recovery steps

Hi Morgan / Acme infra team —

We’re seeing a targeted capacity/egress degradation affecting requests routed to eu-west-1 and ap-southeast-1 in the last 45 minutes. Symptoms we observe: elevated 5xx rate (predominantly 502/504), increased p99 latency for generation calls, and intermittent KV fetch timeouts for a subset of hosts. Impact is limited to a subset of customers behind specific cloud load balancer paths; US regions are nominal right now.

Immediate mitigation steps (what you can do right now):
- Prefer region override: set inference.region=us-west-2 or us-east-1 in your Redwood client to avoid eu/apac endpoints for the next 2–4 hours.
- Enable fallback model: if you have model_fallback configured, temporarily permit "redwood-compact-v1" as a lower-latency fallback to reduce retries.
- Increase client timeout to 30s (was 15s) and retry with exponential backoff (max 2 retries) to avoid duplicate completions.
- Avoid large batch windows; switch to smaller batch_size (<=8) to reduce queueing pressure.

We’re pushing a targeted traffic-steer at our edge to move new connections away from the affected POPs, but some in-flight sessions may still hit degraded hosts — the region override is the fastest customer-facing control.

Attachments: incident-summary-20281002.pdf (short summary) and a small trace snapshot for affected APAC hosts.

I’ll follow up with ETA and root-cause once the mitigation stabilizes.

—Camila
Camila Reyes
Account Lead, Americas
Redwood Inference

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>, Morgan Chen <morgan@datasurge.com>, Acme AI <infra@acme.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 02 Oct 2028 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Targeted region surge — immediate traffic steering advice & recovery steps

Morgan —
Short answers:
- Billing: routing to a fallback model variant counts per-token on the model variant used. If you enable redwood-compact-v1 as fallback, tokens consumed there will be billed at that variant’s rate. We can work a temporary credit if this materially impacts you; Camila will handle credits.
- Embedding cache: prefix/KV cache hits remain served from the cache if the cache layer is local to the application or via our global CDN. If your cache lookup is a separate request that would have hit apac/eu, steering away means you’ll see cache-miss patterns only if cache was region-bound.

Operational notes from our side: we’re performing a micro-flow drain of 3 edge hosts in eu-west-1 and 2 hosts in ap-southeast-1 showing abnormal TCP retransmit rates; new sessions are being diverted to us-east-1/us-west-2. Expect gradual recovery in 30–90 minutes as hosts rejoin rotation.

For now: set timeouts, enable fallback, and reduce batch size. We’ll provide a public status update in 15 minutes with the incident ID.

—Ethan
Ethan Park
Senior Incident Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Morgan Chen <morgan@datasurge.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Oct 2028 10:08:00 -0700

Adding technical detail / small remediation steps for platform engineers at Acme:

title: Sunrise Analytics — Request for contractual exceptions & pricing approval
preview:
From: Olive Spencer <olive@sunrise-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, procure@sunrise-analytics.com
Date: Thu, 20 Sep 2028 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Request: pricing + two contractual asks for quick close

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — team was impressed with the latency and the dedicated pool options. We want to move fast. Two items from our side before we can sign:

1) Pricing: Sunrise is asking for a one-time first-year volume discount of 30% on usage above the committed tier in year one (we project ~1.2x the committed tokens).
2) Contract language: Procurement has flagged two redlines they need addressed before procurement can approve the PO:
   - Modify indemnity to carve out third-party IP claims arising from our customers' data processing (they want a limited reciprocal carveout).
   - Increase liability cap above standard to a mutually agreed $5M for the initial 12-month term.

I attached our RFP and the redlined document our GC prepared (SRA-Proposed-Redlines.docx). Can Redwood accommodate? We are ready to sign if we get a pricing and legal path within 72 hours.

Best,
Olive
Olive Spencer
Head of Platform Partnerships, Sunrise Analytics
olive@sunrise-analytics.com
(415) 555-0198

Attachments:
- Sunrise_RFP_2028.pdf
- SRA-Proposed-Redlines.docx


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 Sep 2028 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: pricing + two contractual asks for quick close

Team — forwarding Sunrise's asks. Quick summary: they want a one-time 30% discount on year-one overage, an indemnity carveout for third-party IP related to customer data, and a higher liability cap at $5M for 12 months. They claim fast close if we can agree within 72h.

Can legal weigh in on the indemnity and cap? Deal desk / finance, can we support the 30% first-year overage discount as a one-time concession? If exec signoff is needed, call out exactly what language and approval level you need.

I'll hold off replying to Olive until we have a clear ask/document to send.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive — Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com

On Thu, 20 Sep 2028 at 09:12 AM Olive Spencer <olive@sunrise-analytics.com> wrote:
> (original message attached)


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Sep 2028 15:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: pricing + two contractual asks for quick close

Ava / team — I reviewed the SRA-Proposed-Redlines.docx. Summary and recommendation below.

Summary of material requests:
- Indemnity: Sunrise is asking for a carveout that would limit Redwood's ability to seek indemnification for customer-originated IP claims. The proposed language shifts risk substantially to Redwood and removes some of our typical protections.
- Liability Cap: They want the cap raised from our standard 1x ARR (or contract value) to $5M for the initial 12-month term.

Legal assessment and options:
1) Indemnity: We cannot accept the proposed broad carveout as written. We can offer a narrow, reciprocal carveout limited to third-party IP claims directly resulting from the customer's use of the service with customer-provided models or content, provided Sunrise accepts a standard defense/notice carve-in and a materiality threshold.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither party shall be responsible for claims arising solely from the other party's data, configurations, or customer-provided models, except where such data was materially altered by the indemnified party's willful misconduct."

title: Priority eviction policy & queue weighting for Dedicated pools
preview:
From: Sasha Ivanov <sasha@vectorlabs.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@vectorlabs.com
Date: Mon, 18 Sep 2028 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Dedicated pool: eviction priority / queue weighting questions

Hey Alex,

Quick follow up from our internal architecture review — we want to lock down how Redwood will handle ephemeral/headroom capacity and preemptible GPUs for the Dedicated pool in the VectorLabs deal. Two specific asks:

1) Eviction ordering and notification: what gets evicted first (ephemeral jobs, low-priority background requests, or live inference flows)? Do we provide any preemption notice callbacks so we can gracefully drain sessions?

2) Queue weighting / priority lanes: we have three traffic classes (interactive low-latency, batch/async pipeline, and background retraining). We need a concrete priority matrix and expected SLOs during a transient surge (expected peak ~220k tokens/sec across models, steady ~45k).

We’re looking at a committed baseline of 40 A100-equivalent GPUs with shoulder capacity for burst. We need to understand: allowed percent of preemptible capacity, typical preemption windows, and the commercial crediting model tied to preemption or throttling.

Can you share a document that shows the eviction priority matrix, the autoscale cooldowns/step sizes, and sample SLA language that covers credits during preemptions? Procurement copied on this thread is keen to review.

Thanks — Sasha
Sasha Ivanov
Head of Platform Engineering, VectorLabs

---
Attachment: system-traffic-estimates.csv (referenced)

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Sasha Ivanov <sasha@vectorlabs.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, procurement@vectorlabs.com
Date: Mon, 18 Sep 2028 18:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Dedicated pool: eviction priority / queue weighting questions

Thanks Sasha — good set of questions. I put together an initial priority/eviction outline and a simple queue-weighting approach; attaching a short matrix (priority_matrix.xlsx) and a one-page summary below. This is the starting point — Karthik and Marcus, please weigh in on technical and contract implications.

Summary (high level):
- Lanes: Reserved (commit), Shoulder (pay-as-you-burst up to X), Ephemeral (preemptible spot-like pool).
- Eviction ordering (highest -> lowest priority to keep): Reserved traffic -> Shoulder high-priority -> Shoulder low-priority -> Ephemeral.
- Preemption notice: 30s lightweight signal + 5m graceful-drain webhook support for interactive sessions (we can extend to 15m for long-running jobs by contract).
- Queue weighting: use exponential decay weights with 3 pools mapped to the traffic classes; interactive gets 60% of scoring weight, batch 30%, background 10% during contention. We’ll provide runtime knobs per-route.
- Autoscale defaults: scale step = 10% of pool size, cooldown = 90s, max burst = 50% of committed baseline (subject to capacity).

Next actions: Karthik to confirm preemption behavior and cache handling; Marcus to propose commercial limits (allowed preemptible % and crediting). I’ll prepare a draft SLA and include the eviction matrix.

Regards,
Alex Martinez
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference

Attachment: priority_matrix.xlsx (stub)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Sasha Ivanov <sasha@vectorlabs.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Sep 2028 09:38:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Dedicated pool: eviction priority / queue weighting questions

High-level technical follow up from infra side. A few concrete constraints and recommended configs to make the behavior predictable and safe:

1) Preemptible pool behavior:
- Default preemptible cap: 20% of total committed GPUs. We can increase to 30% for a negotiated fee.
- Eviction order: tasks on preemptible GPUs are first to be reclaimed. Within preemptible, idle/queued jobs are reclaimed before active inferences with active SSE (session) tokens.
- Preemption notice: we will emit a short GRPC/webhook 30s signal and an optional 5-minute drain window for sessions. For long-running jobs (large-batch training or very long-gen), we recommend 15-minute notice as part of the shoulder contract.

2) Autoscaling policy (recommended defaults):
- Metric: 95th-percentile request-queue-time + GPU util delta triggers.
- Scale up: add min(8 GPUs, 10% of pool) every 45s if sustained over 2 datapoints (2 x 45s).
- Scale down: remove 5% of pool after 10 minutes under low-util. Use staggered removal to avoid cache-thrashing.
- Cooldown window: 5 minutes after a scale event before reversing.

3) Cache and model fallback:
- When preemption or eviction occurs we will attempt to persist KV cache to a short-lived LRU store for 10 minutes to reduce cold-start token cost. If cold-start occurs, route to lighter quantized variant (q4_8b) automatically as fallback.

4) Observability:
- We will surface preemption events to your telemetry stream and expose a preemption_count metric and average_drain_time.

Note on cost & risk: increasing preemptible cap reduces committed cost but increases cold-start probability for interactive sessions. Recommend baseline 40 GPUs, shoulder up to +20 (burst to 60) and preemptible 15-20% for initial contract.

-Karthik
Principal Infra Eng, Redwood Inference

Thanks — commercial notes and constraints:

- Credit model: propose SLA credit bands tied to sustained preemption-caused error rate on the interactive route. Example: >1% user-visible error over a 1h window => 5% monthly credit; >3% => 15% credit.
- Allowed preemptible percentage: default 20% in the SOW. If VectorLabs wants 30% we need a higher burst premium and reduced SLA tiers.
- Shoulder billing: pay-as-you-burst will be invoiced at 1.6x hosted per-GPU-minute for shoulder minutes beyond baseline. We can cap monthly shoulder spend if needed.
- Contract language: we should call out a max preemption notice of 5 minutes for interactive routes, and a guaranteed warm-pool of 5 GPUs for Reserved traffic to reduce cold starts.

I’ll draft the commercial bullets and fold into the proposed-sla.pdf. Monica, please check that our suggested credit bands are within standard precedence.

~Marcus

- Credit model: propose SLA credit bands tied to sustained preemption-caused error rate on the interactive route. Example: >1% user-visible error over a 1h window => 5% monthly credit; >3% => 15% credit.
- Allowed preemptible percentage: default 20% in the SOW. If VectorLabs wants 30% we need a higher burst premium and reduced SLA tiers.
- Shoulder billing: pay-as-you-burst will be invoiced at 1.6x hosted per-GPU-minute for shoulder minutes beyond baseline. We can cap monthly shoulder spend if needed.
- Contract language: we should call out a max preemption notice of 5 minutes for interactive routes, and a guaranteed warm-pool of 5 GPUs for Reserved traffic to reduce cold starts.

I’ll draft the commercial bullets and fold into the proposed-sla.pdf. Monica, please check that our suggested credit bands are within standard precedence.

~Marcus
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference

---

> Note on cost & risk: increasing preemptible cap reduces committed cost but increases cold-start probability for interactive sessions. Recommend baseline 40 GPUs, shoulder up to +20 (burst to 60) and preemptible 15-20% for initial contract.

Subject: Re: Dedicated pool: eviction priority / queue weighting questions

- VectorLabs is comfortable with a 15% preemptible cap to start if we get a 5-minute preemption notice for interactive flows and a guaranteed warm-pool of at least 4 GPUs for Reserved traffic.
- We’d like the eviction priority matrix included verbatim in the SOW and a sample credit clause that mirrors Marcus’ bands.
- Procurement (procurement@vectorlabs.com) will need the formal SOW and a DocuSign-ready SLA by end of next week (EOD Sep 26).

Procurement note: we’ll also run a short smoke test (400k token bursts for 10 minutes) to validate the shoulder behavior before signing. Will Redwood support a 2-hour scheduled test window?

Thanks, all. I’ve consolidated the agreed points and attached a draft SOW + SLA (proposed-sla.pdf) and the priority matrix. Highlights:

- Baseline commit: 40 GPUs. Shoulder: up to +20 (burst to 60). Preemptible pool: 15% of baseline (6 GPUs).
- Preemption & drain: 30s signal + 5-minute grace for interactive; 15-minute for scheduled long jobs if requested in advance.

- Test window: Redwood can support a 2-hour scheduled smoke test; propose Sep 24 02:00-04:00 UTC — confirm if that works for VectorLabs.

Alex Martinez

Carlos — thanks for the quick turnaround and for sending the notarized material. A couple of items from procurement / AP that will determine whether we can release the PO from hold: 

1) Certified English translation: the notary cert is in Spanish. Our AP policy requires a certified translation of any notarial documents not in English. Please attach a certified English translation of the notary page or confirm if the notary will provide an official bilingual version.

2) W-8BEN-E completeness: on page 2, box 9 (Claim of tax treaty benefits / U.S. TIN) is blank — that is expected for most foreign entities without a U.S. EIN, but we need explicit confirmation in the signature block that Sierra Bridge is declaring foreign status and providing the CUIT as the foreign TIN. If you can add a short addendum (one paragraph) certifying the CUIT as foreign TIN and its format, that will help our tax team.

3) Bank routing: AP needs a cleared bank confirmation letter or a bank-attested voided check PDF for the beneficiary details you provided. That can be redacted to show only account name and last four digits if needed.

Priya — looping you in for AP documentation requirements and the translation vendor. Can finance confirm whether Redwood can accept a certified translation produced by an Argentine court translator, or if we should request a translated and notarized version from Sierra Bridge?

Carlos — once we have the translation and the short TIN confirmation addendum I will route to Karthik and legal for conditional PO clearance.

Best,
Alex
Senior Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference

title: Echelon RFP: request for subprocessor roster and planned cloud logging provider consolidation
preview:
From: Evelyn Carter <evelyn.carter@echelon.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: RFP Team <rfp@echelon.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Sep 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFP follow-up: Subprocessor roster + cloud provider change notice

Ben —

Thanks for the initial RFP materials. A couple of clarifications from our security and procurement teams before we proceed to the final evaluation: 1) we need a complete subprocessor roster (names of third-party vendors, primary function, region of processing, and any changes in the last 6 months); 2) your RFI mentions a planned consolidation of logging/telemetry to a new managed provider — can you confirm which vendor will be receiving operational logs and whether any customer data (even metadata/KV cache dumps) may transit that provider?

Please attach any SOC2 summaries or evidence packages you can share under NDA. We need this for our vendor risk scoring and to complete the security-weighted section of the RFP.

Timeline: procurement hopes to have vendor clarifications back by end-of-week (9/14). If that’s tight, let us know the earliest you can provide the roster and the impact memo.

Best,
Evelyn Carter
Vendor Risk Manager
Echelon Financial

Subject: Re: RFP follow-up: Subprocessor roster + cloud provider change notice

Evelyn —

Thanks for the note and the clear asks. I’m looping in Naomi (Head of Security & Compliance) and Karthik (Product) to get you an authoritative roster and the operational impact memo. We can provide an redacted subprocessor roster for initial scoring immediately and a full roster under NDA and customer-specified handling if required.

Quick answer to the logging question: we are migrating telemetry from an in-house Splunk cluster to a managed logging provider (LogStream Managed) for aggregation/alerts only — no persistent customer PII is stored in their tenant. Naomi will confirm technical controls + evidence.

I’ll follow up with the attachments by EOD today. If you need this sooner, tell me and I’ll escalate.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0184

Customer (Echelon Financial) has asked for: full subprocessor roster, SOC2 summary, and confirmation re: logging consolidation to LogStream Managed. Procurement wants answers by 9/14. Date: Mon, 10 Sep 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFP follow-up: Subprocessor roster + cloud provider change notice
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: RFP Team <rfp@echelon.com>

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Carter <evelyn.carter@echelon.com>, RFP Team <rfp@echelon.com>
Date: Tue, 11 Sep 2028 08:32:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: RFP follow-up: Subprocessor roster + cloud provider change notice

Ben / Karthik / Evelyn —

Apologies for the slight delay; below are the consolidated answers and the attachments requested. I’ve included internal notes where we recommend NDA-first sharing.

1) Subprocessor roster (redacted public version attached — subprocessor_roster_2028Q3.pdf): includes vendor name, primary function, region of processing, and SOC/ISO attestations where available. Full roster (incl. contract references and assigned subprocessors) is provided under NDA only.

2) Logging consolidation: the migration is from an on-prem Splunk index to LogStream Managed (US region). Important points:
- What moves: aggregated telemetry (metrics, application logs) and anonymized service diagnostics.
- What does NOT move: any persistent customer PII, KV cache snapshots, or any persisted LLM context data. Token-level or prompt-level content is never routed to LogStream.
- Controls: TLS-in-transit, customer-data redaction pipeline (applies to structured fields we know as potentially sensitive), and role-based access with MFA for LogStream accounts.

3) Evidence included: Redwood SOC2 summary (redwood-soc2-summary-2028.pdf) and an operational impact memo describing end-to-end flow (cloud_change_operational_impact_memo.pdf). See the memos for retention windows and purge controls.

4) Timeline & commitments: we can provide the public roster now; we can execute an NDA and deliver the full roster + contract excerpts within 2 business days of NDA execution. If Echelon requires additional auditing access or a provider-specific attestation from LogStream, we can coordinate but that will take 7–10 business days.

If you’d like, we can also provide a short 30-minute tech deep-dive with our infra team to walk through telemetry sanitization filters.

Regards,
Naomi Feldman
Head of Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference
naomi@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

[Attachment: subprocessor_roster_2028Q3.pdf (PDF, 434KB)]
[Attachment: cloud_change_operational_impact_memo.pdf (PDF, 218KB)]
[Attachment: redwood-soc2-summary-2028.pdf (PDF, 120KB)]

Thanks for the quick review. Attached are the public subprocessor roster and the operational impact memo and SOC2 summary Naomi mentioned. We’ve highlighted the LogStream sections in the memo; to reiterate, no customer prompt content or KV cache snapshots are forwarded to LogStream.

Next steps: 1) if you want the full roster and contract references we can send an NDA template (I’ve attached ours and can accept Echelon’s if preferred); 2) Naomi or Karthik are available Thursday or Friday for a 30-minute technical call.

I’ll follow up with the attachments by EOD today. If you need this sooner, tell me and I’ll escalate.

Ben / Karthik / Evelyn —

Apologies for the slight delay; below are the consolidated answers and the attachments requested. I’ve included internal notes where we recommend NDA-first sharing.

1) Subprocessor roster (redacted public version attached — subprocessor_roster_2028Q3.pdf): includes vendor name, primary function, region of processing, and SOC/ISO attestations where available. Full roster (incl. contract references and assigned subprocessors) is provided under NDA only.

2) Logging consolidation: the migration is from an on-prem Splunk index to LogStream Managed (US region). Important points:
- What moves: aggregated telemetry (metrics, application logs) and anonymized service diagnostics.
- What does NOT move: any persistent customer PII, KV cache snapshots, or any persisted LLM context data. Token-level or prompt-level content is never routed to LogStream.
- Controls: TLS-in-transit, customer-data redaction pipeline (applies to structured fields we know as potentially sensitive), and role-based access with MFA for LogStream accounts.

3) Evidence included: Redwood SOC2 summary (redwood-soc2-summary-2028.pdf) and an operational impact memo describing end-to-end flow (cloud_change_operational_impact_memo.pdf). See the memos for retention windows and purge controls.

4) Timeline & commitments: we can provide the public roster now; we can execute an NDA and deliver the full roster + contract excerpts within 2 business days of NDA execution. If Echelon requires additional auditing access or a provider-specific attestation from LogStream, we can coordinate but that will take 7–10 business days.

If you’d like, we can also provide a short 30-minute tech deep-dive with our infra team to walk through telemetry sanitization filters.

Regards,

title: Contract term refresh — SLO uplift & ops governance
preview:
From: Emma Zhao <emma.zhao@stratusbank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@stratusbank.com
Date: Tue, 5 Sep 2028 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Contract and SLO discussion — StratusBank renewal

Hi Ben,

Hope you’re well. We’re approaching the end of our current term and StratusBank is evaluating an upgrade to a stronger operational commitment. Two asks from our side: a tighter P95/P99 latency SLO for production endpoints (we'd like p95 < 120ms / p99 < 400ms for typical transaction workloads) and access to a Premium+ support path with faster response targets and a named technical account lead.

High-level context: over the past 12 months our peak usage has grown ~3.8x and we’re planning a feature expansion that will increase concurrent sessions by ~40%. We want to lock predictable latency and a clear escalation path for the new product.

Can you share a recommended SLO upgrade, estimated uplift to committed capacity, and any governance language you’d need in the contract to formalize a premium SLA? I’ve attached our renewal request summary.

Thanks,
Emma
Emma Zhao
Head of Platform Engineering
StratusBank

Attachments: StratusBank_Renewal_Request.pdf (pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Emma Zhao <emma.zhao@stratusbank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 6 Sep 2028 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract and SLO discussion — StratusBank renewal

Emma —

Thanks for the clear context and the numbers. We can absolutely put together a Premium SLA package that targets the latency band you described. Quick summary of what I’m proposing for an initial offer: 

- Premium Plus SLO: p95 < 110ms, p99 < 350ms for the primary inference endpoints (applies to 95% of traffic slices).
- Dedicated capacity reservation: baseline commit for a 40% throughput uplift from current peak; burst pool for seasonal peaks.
- Named TAM + 4-hour critical response, 1-business-hour P1 triage window.
- Governance: quarterly joint review, runbook access, and a mutually agreed Mutually Action Plan for cutover and capacity changes.

I’ve attached two stubs: a short SLO tier doc and a mutual action plan template. Pricing options will vary by term length and commit — Marissa, can you prepare 12/24/36-month options and an onboarding credit for StratusBank? Aditya, I’ve looped you on the technical constraints we should validate (KV cache sizing, heartbeats, and tail-latency mitigation).

Proposed next step: 30-minute call this week to align on exact SLO language and capacity numbers so we can get legal drafting started.

Best,
Ben
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0112

Attachments: Proposed_SLO_Tiers.pdf (pdf); Mutual_Action_Plan.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Emma Zhao <emma.zhao@stratusbank.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 7 Sep 2028 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract and SLO discussion — StratusBank renewal

Emma — thanks for the detail. A few technical notes to make sure expectations align before we lock language: 

1) Workload profile: please confirm 95th/99th percentiles are measured on end-to-end inference time (gateway -> model -> response). If you have a trace sample for a representative day, that helps us size the commit precisely.

2) Tail mitigation: for p99 targets we recommend:
   - Dedicated GPU pool with KV prefix caching for warm sessions
   - Continuous batching policy tuned for 10–30ms batching window for inference bursts
   - Optional quantization profile if you accept the small QAT delta for lower token compute

3) Operational controls: we can include a fallback routing policy to degrade to a lower-cost variant if capacity saturates; this can be gated by customer KPs in the MAP.

I’ll run a quick cost sensitivity with Marissa once we have your trace. Proposed times for the alignment call: Thu 9/7 11:00am PT or Fri 9/8 9:00am PT.

—Aditya
Sr. Product Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com


From: Emma Zhao <emma.zhao@stratusbank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: David Carlson <david.carlson@stratusbank.com>, procurement@stratusbank.com
Date: Tue, 12 Sep 2028 11:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract and SLO discussion — StratusBank renewal

Ben / Aditya —

Thanks for the quick reply and the detailed notes (Aditya — the quantization note is especially helpful). We ran a small tracer on last Tuesday’s traffic (attached summary) and confirmed the current p95 ~ 180ms and p99 ~ 620ms during peak windows — so your proposed SLOs would be a meaningful uplift.

Procurement / legal questions we want to resolve before internal approval:
- Term length: preference for 24 or 36 months if the unit economics improve with a longer commit.
- Credits/penalties: sample SLA credits for missed monthly p99 targets.
- Evidence: we will need SOC2 attestation and a 30-day audit window for logs.

Looping David (procurement) — David, can you confirm the internal timeline for signature? We’re hoping to finalize terms and sign by the end of October to align with internal budgeting.

Best,
Emma

Attachment: StratusBank_traffic_sample_20280905.pdf (pdf)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Emma Zhao <emma.zhao@stratusbank.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Sep 2028 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract and SLO discussion — StratusBank renewal

Emma —

Appreciate the sample. Attached are three pricing pathways that map to the SLO/commit bands we discussed: 12m, 24m, 36m. High level: 24m gives ~12% unit discount vs 12m and includes a one-time onboarding credit (equivalent to 1 month of baseline compute). 36m increases the commit discount to ~18% and includes an elevated burst buffer for two planned spikes per year.

SLA language notes: we’re prepared to include a formal credit table tied to monthly p99 targets, with a safety cap and an escalation clause that triggers an emergency resync plan and a named remediation timeline. For SOC2 and evidence requests, we’ll provide the attestation and a 30-day read-only audit window — we’ll coordinate with our security team and legal.

Next steps:
- Ben: can you lock the mutual action plan dates and spin a short doc that lists the cutover milestones?
- Irene: please review the proposed SLA credit table below and confirm if the language is OK for a standard addendum.

I’m available for a 30-min call Mon 9/18 or Tue 9/19 to finalize pricing and SLA wording.

Cheers,
Marissa Cole
Director, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

Attachments: StratusBank_Pricing_Options.xlsx (xlsx)


From: David Carlson <david.carlson@stratusbank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@stratusbank.com, Emma Zhao <emma.zhao@stratusbank.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Sep 2028 22:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract and SLO discussion — StratusBank renewal

Ben —

Procurement here. We like the 24-month economics but need one item before budget approval: legal wants a clearer remediation clause tied to measurable KPIs (we prefer explicit credit % tied to monthly p99 breaches). Also please confirm the onboarding timeline and whether you can provide a 2-week ramp-up runbook with named contacts.

If you can send a redline-ready SLA addendum and the runbook by next Thu 9/21, we’ll aim to get internal sign-off and circulate a PO.

Thanks,
David Carlson
Head of Procurement | StratusBank
